name: Ada Lovelace
taglineCn: 世界第一位计算机程序员
bio: Augusta Ada King, Countess of Lovelace, recognized that Charles Babbage's proposed Analytical Engine had potential far beyond mere calculation. In 1843, she published extensive notes on the engine that included what is now recognized as the first algorithm intended for machine processing — making her the world's first computer programmer. Her visionary insight that machines could manipulate symbols according to rules anticipated modern computing by a full century.
bioCn: 阿达·洛芙莱斯，洛芙莱斯伯爵夫人，认识到查尔斯·巴贝奇提出的分析机的潜力远不止简单的计算。1843年，她发表了关于分析机的详细注释，其中包含了现在被公认为第一个为机器处理而设计的算法——使她成为世界上第一位计算机程序员。她关于机器可以按规则操纵符号的远见卓识，提前整整一个世纪预见了现代计算。
achievements: Published the first computer algorithm
Envisioned general-purpose computing a century ahead
First to recognize machines could process more than numbers
achievementsCn: 发表了第一个计算机算法
提前一个世纪预见了通用计算
第一个认识到机器可以处理数字以外的事物
quote: That brain of mine is something more than merely mortal, as time will show.
quoteCn: 我的大脑不仅仅是凡人的大脑，时间会证明这一点。
references: Toole, Betty A. Ada, the Enchantress of Numbers: Poetical Science. Strawberry Press, 1998.
Lovelace, A. 'Notes on L. Menabrea's Sketch of the Analytical Engine,' 1843.

name: Grace Hopper
taglineCn: 计算机编程语言先驱
bio: Rear Admiral Grace Murray Hopper was a mathematician and U.S. Navy officer who revolutionized computer programming. She developed the first compiler, which translated written language into computer code, and led the team that created COBOL, one of the first modern programming languages. Her insistence that programming languages should be closer to English transformed the accessibility of computing for generations.
bioCn: 海军少将格蕾丝·穆雷·霍珀是一位数学家和美国海军军官，她彻底改变了计算机编程。她开发了第一个编译器，将书面语言翻译成计算机代码，并领导团队创建了COBOL——最早的现代编程语言之一。她坚持认为编程语言应该更接近英语的理念，改变了几代人接触计算的方式。
achievements: Developed the first compiler (A-0 System)
Led creation of COBOL programming language
Popularized the term 'debugging'
Reached rank of Rear Admiral in U.S. Navy
achievementsCn: 开发了第一个编译器（A-0系统）
领导创建了COBOL编程语言
普及了'调试（debugging）'一词
晋升为美国海军少将
quote: The most dangerous phrase in the language is, 'We've always done it this way.'
quoteCn: 语言中最危险的一句话是：'我们一直都是这样做的。'
references: Beyer, Kurt W. Grace Hopper and the Invention of the Information Age. MIT Press, 2009.
Yale University: Grace Murray Hopper Collection

name: Betty Holberton
taglineCn: 发明断点调试的ENIAC程序员
bio: Betty Holberton was one of the six original programmers of ENIAC, the world's first general-purpose electronic computer. She wrote the first software application, invented breakpoints for debugging, and helped develop the UNIVAC. She also played a key role in developing the first sort-merge generator and contributed to the creation of COBOL and Fortran standards.
bioCn: 贝蒂·霍尔伯顿是世界上第一台通用电子计算机ENIAC的六位原始程序员之一。她编写了第一个软件应用程序，发明了用于调试的断点，并帮助开发了UNIVAC。她还在开发第一个排序合并生成器方面发挥了关键作用，并为COBOL和Fortran标准的创建做出了贡献。
achievements: One of six original ENIAC programmers
Invented breakpoints for debugging
Helped develop COBOL and Fortran standards
achievementsCn: 六位ENIAC原始程序员之一
发明了用于调试的断点
帮助开发了COBOL和Fortran标准
references: Kleiman, Kathy. Proving Ground: The Untold Story of the Six Women Who Programmed the World's First Modern Computer. Grand Central, 2022.

name: Jean Jennings Bartik
taglineCn: ENIAC先驱与计算远见者
bio: Jean Jennings Bartik was one of the original programmers of ENIAC. Born on a farm in Missouri, she studied mathematics and was hired to calculate ballistic trajectories during World War II. She went on to help convert ENIAC into a stored-program computer and later worked on BINAC and UNIVAC. Her contributions were overlooked for decades until she was inducted into the Women in Technology International Hall of Fame in 1997.
bioCn: 琼·詹宁斯·巴蒂克是ENIAC的原始程序员之一。她出生在密苏里州的一个农场，学习数学后在二战期间被聘用计算弹道轨迹。她后来帮助将ENIAC转换为存储程序计算机，并参与了BINAC和UNIVAC的开发。她的贡献被忽视了数十年，直到1997年入选国际女性技术名人堂。
achievements: One of six original ENIAC programmers
Helped convert ENIAC to stored-program computer
Worked on BINAC and UNIVAC
achievementsCn: 六位ENIAC原始程序员之一
帮助将ENIAC转换为存储程序计算机
参与BINAC和UNIVAC开发
references: Bartik, Jean. Pioneer Programmer: Jean Jennings Bartik and the Computer That Changed the World. Truman State Univ Press, 2013.

name: Kathleen McNulty
taglineCn: ENIAC程序员与数学计算员
bio: Kathleen McNulty Mauchly Antonelli was born in Ireland and emigrated to the United States as a child. One of the six original ENIAC programmers, she used her mathematics degree to compute ballistic firing tables during World War II. She later married ENIAC co-inventor John Mauchly and continued to contribute to the development of computing technology throughout her life.
bioCn: 凯瑟琳·麦克纳尔蒂·莫奇利·安东内利出生于爱尔兰，童年时移居美国。作为ENIAC六位原始程序员之一，她利用数学学位在二战期间计算弹道射击表。她后来嫁给了ENIAC的共同发明人约翰·莫奇利，并在一生中继续为计算技术的发展做出贡献。
achievements: One of six original ENIAC programmers
Computed ballistic firing tables during WWII
Contributed to early computing development
achievementsCn: 六位ENIAC原始程序员之一
二战期间计算弹道射击表
为早期计算发展做出贡献
references: Kleiman, Kathy. Proving Ground. Grand Central, 2022.

name: Frances Allen
tagline: First Woman to Win the Turing Award
taglineCn: 首位女性图灵奖得主
bio: Frances Allen spent her career at IBM pioneering compiler optimization and parallelization. Her seminal 1966 paper on program optimization laid the theoretical foundation for modern compilers. In 2006, she became the first woman to receive the A.M. Turing Award, computing's highest honor, for her contributions to the theory and practice of optimizing compiler techniques.
bioCn: 弗朗西丝·艾伦在IBM度过了她的职业生涯，开创了编译器优化和并行化的先河。她1966年发表的关于程序优化的开创性论文为现代编译器奠定了理论基础。2006年，她因对优化编译器技术的理论和实践的贡献，成为第一位获得图灵奖——计算机领域最高荣誉——的女性。
achievements: First woman to receive the Turing Award (2006)
Pioneered compiler optimization theory
Foundational work on parallelization at IBM
achievementsCn: 首位女性图灵奖得主（2006年）
开创编译器优化理论
在IBM进行并行化基础工作
references: ACM Turing Award: Frances E. Allen — https://amturing.acm.org/award_winners/allen_1012327.cfm
Allen, F.E. 'Program Optimization,' Annual Review in Automatic Programming, 1969.

name: Margaret Hamilton
taglineCn: 编写了登月代码
bio: Margaret Hamilton led the team that developed the on-board flight software for NASA's Apollo missions. Her rigorous approach to software engineering — a term she coined — ensured the Apollo 11 mission's success when her error-detection code prevented an abort during the lunar landing. The iconic photograph of her standing next to a stack of Apollo guidance code taller than herself has become a symbol of women in computing.
bioCn: 玛格丽特·汉密尔顿领导团队开发了NASA阿波罗任务的机载飞行软件。她严谨的软件工程方法——这个术语正是她创造的——确保了阿波罗11号任务的成功，她的错误检测代码在登月过程中阻止了任务中止。她站在比自己还高的阿波罗导航代码旁边的标志性照片，已经成为计算机领域女性的象征。
achievements: Led Apollo on-board flight software development
Coined the term 'software engineering'
Received Presidential Medal of Freedom (2016)
achievementsCn: 领导阿波罗机载飞行软件开发
创造了'软件工程'一词
获得总统自由勋章（2016年）
quote: There was no choice but to be pioneers.
quoteCn: 我们别无选择，只能成为先驱。
quoteSource: On the Apollo program, from interviews

name: Radia Perlman
taglineCn: 互联网之母
bio: Radia Perlman's invention of the Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) was fundamental to the operation of network bridges and made today's internet possible. Often called the 'Mother of the Internet,' she has made lasting contributions to network design and security, including the TRILL protocol. She holds over 100 patents and continues to shape how data moves across global networks.
bioCn: 拉迪亚·珀尔曼发明的生成树协议（STP）是网络桥接操作的基础，使今天的互联网成为可能。她常被称为'互联网之母'，在网络设计和安全方面做出了持久的贡献，包括TRILL协议。她拥有100多项专利，继续影响着数据在全球网络中的传输方式。
achievements: Invented Spanning Tree Protocol (STP)
Holds over 100 patents in networking
Developed TRILL protocol
achievementsCn: 发明了生成树协议（STP）
在网络领域拥有100多项专利
开发了TRILL协议
references: Perlman, Radia. Interconnections: Bridges, Routers, Switches. Addison-Wesley, 1999.

name: Barbara Liskov
tagline: Turing Award Winner, Liskov Substitution Principle
taglineCn: 图灵奖得主，里氏替换原则
bio: Barbara Liskov is a computer scientist whose work on programming language design, software engineering methodology, and distributed systems earned her the 2008 Turing Award. She is best known for the Liskov Substitution Principle, a fundamental concept in object-oriented programming. She was one of the first women in the United States to earn a PhD in computer science.
bioCn: 芭芭拉·利斯科夫是一位计算机科学家，她在编程语言设计、软件工程方法和分布式系统方面的工作使她获得了2008年图灵奖。她最著名的是里氏替换原则，这是面向对象编程中的基本概念。她是美国最早获得计算机科学博士学位的女性之一。
achievements: Turing Award recipient (2008)
Formulated the Liskov Substitution Principle
Pioneered data abstraction in programming
achievementsCn: 图灵奖得主（2008年）
提出里氏替换原则
开创了编程中的数据抽象

name: Shafi Goldwasser
taglineCn: 现代密码学先驱
bio: Shafi Goldwasser co-invented probabilistic encryption and zero-knowledge proofs, two cornerstones of modern cryptography. Her work laid the mathematical foundations for secure digital communication. She received the Turing Award in 2012, becoming only the third woman to receive the honor, alongside two Gödel Prizes and numerous other accolades.
bioCn: 沙菲·戈德瓦塞尔共同发明了概率加密和零知识证明，这是现代密码学的两大基石。她的工作为安全数字通信奠定了数学基础。她在2012年获得图灵奖，成为第三位获此殊荣的女性，此外还获得了两项哥德尔奖和众多其他荣誉。
achievements: Turing Award recipient (2012)
Co-invented probabilistic encryption
Pioneered zero-knowledge proofs
achievementsCn: 图灵奖得主（2012年）
共同发明概率加密
开创零知识证明

name: Fei-Fei Li
tagline: Pioneer of Computer Vision and ImageNet
taglineCn: 计算机视觉与ImageNet先驱
bio: Fei-Fei Li is a computer scientist who transformed the field of artificial intelligence through ImageNet, a massive visual database that catalyzed the deep learning revolution. Her work demonstrated that AI systems need large, diverse datasets to learn effectively. As a professor at Stanford and former chief scientist at Google Cloud, she advocates for human-centered AI and diversity in technology.
bioCn: 李飞飞是一位计算机科学家，通过ImageNet——一个催化深度学习革命的大型视觉数据库——改变了人工智能领域。她的工作表明，AI系统需要大规模、多样化的数据集来有效学习。作为斯坦福大学教授和前谷歌云首席科学家，她倡导以人为本的AI和技术多样性。
achievements: Created ImageNet, catalyzing the deep learning revolution
Stanford professor and AI research leader
Advocates for human-centered AI
achievementsCn: 创建ImageNet，催化深度学习革命
斯坦福大学教授和AI研究领导者
倡导以人为本的AI
quote: If we want machines to think, we need to teach them to see.
quoteCn: 如果我们想让机器思考，就需要教它们看见。
references: Deng et al. ImageNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Image Database. CVPR, 2009.

name: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller
tagline: First American Woman with a Computer Science PhD
taglineCn: 美国首位计算机科学女博士
bio: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller was a Catholic nun who became one of the first people in America — and the first woman — to earn a PhD in computer science in 1965. She contributed to the development of the BASIC programming language at Dartmouth College, which was originally men-only but made an exception for her. She founded the computer science department at Clarke College and championed computing education for all.
bioCn: 玛丽·肯尼斯·凯勒修女是一位天主教修女，1965年成为美国最早获得计算机科学博士学位的人之一——也是第一位女性。她在达特茅斯学院参与了BASIC编程语言的开发，该学院原本只收男生，但为她破了例。她在克拉克学院创建了计算机科学系，倡导面向所有人的计算机教育。
achievements: First American woman with a PhD in Computer Science (1965)
Contributed to developing BASIC programming language
Founded computer science department at Clarke College
achievementsCn: 美国首位计算机科学女博士（1965年）
参与开发BASIC编程语言
在克拉克学院创建计算机科学系

name: Adele Goldberg
taglineCn: Smalltalk编程语言共同开发者
bio: Adele Goldberg worked at Xerox PARC where she co-developed the Smalltalk programming language and contributed to the graphical user interface concepts that Steve Jobs famously saw during his 1979 visit. Her work on object-oriented programming and interactive development environments influenced generations of software design, from the Macintosh to modern IDEs.
bioCn: 阿黛尔·戈德堡在施乐帕洛阿尔托研究中心（Xerox PARC）工作，共同开发了Smalltalk编程语言，并为图形用户界面概念做出了贡献——这正是史蒂夫·乔布斯1979年访问时著名地看到的东西。她在面向对象编程和交互式开发环境方面的工作影响了几代软件设计，从Macintosh到现代IDE。
achievements: Co-developed Smalltalk at Xerox PARC
Influenced graphical user interface design
Pioneered object-oriented programming education
achievementsCn: 在施乐PARC共同开发Smalltalk
影响了图形用户界面设计
开创面向对象编程教育
references: Goldberg, A. & Robson, D. Smalltalk-80: The Language. Addison-Wesley, 1989.

name: Karen Spärck Jones
tagline: Pioneer of Information Retrieval and Search Engines
taglineCn: 信息检索与搜索引擎先驱
bio: Karen Spärck Jones was a British computer scientist who developed the concept of inverse document frequency (IDF), a foundational technique now used by virtually every search engine in the world. Working at the Cambridge Computer Laboratory, she bridged the gap between linguistics and computing, establishing the field of natural language processing. Her famous dictum — 'Computing is too important to be left to men' — remains a rallying cry for diversity in tech.
bioCn: 凯伦·斯帕克·琼斯是一位英国计算机科学家，她提出了逆文档频率（IDF）的概念，这一基础技术现在被全世界几乎所有搜索引擎使用。在剑桥计算机实验室工作期间，她架起了语言学和计算之间的桥梁，建立了自然语言处理领域。她的名言——'计算太重要了，不能只留给男人'——至今仍是科技多元化的号角。
achievements: Developed inverse document frequency (IDF)
Pioneer of natural language processing
Bridged linguistics and computer science
achievementsCn: 开发逆文档频率（IDF）
自然语言处理先驱
架起语言学和计算机科学的桥梁
quote: Computing is too important to be left to men.
quoteCn: 计算太重要了，不能只留给男人。
references: Spärck Jones, K. 'A statistical interpretation of term specificity,' Journal of Documentation, 1972.

name: Lynn Conway
taglineCn: 革新了超大规模集成电路设计
bio: Lynn Conway revolutionized microchip design through her work on VLSI (Very Large Scale Integration) technology at Xerox PARC. The Mead-Conway approach she co-developed democratized chip design, enabling thousands of engineers to create custom chips. She also made key contributions to superscalar architecture at IBM. Conway is recognized both for her technical achievements and as a transgender pioneer who rebuilt her career after being fired from IBM in 1968.
bioCn: 林恩·康威通过在施乐PARC进行的VLSI（超大规模集成）技术研究，彻底改变了微芯片设计。她共同开发的Mead-Conway方法使芯片设计民主化，使数千名工程师能够创建定制芯片。她还在IBM对超标量架构做出了关键贡献。康威因其技术成就以及作为跨性别先驱——她在1968年被IBM解雇后重新建立了自己的事业——而受到认可。
achievements: Revolutionized VLSI chip design
Co-developed Mead-Conway approach
Key contributions to superscalar architecture
achievementsCn: 革新了VLSI芯片设计
共同开发Mead-Conway方法
对超标量架构做出关键贡献
references: Mead, C. & Conway, L. Introduction to VLSI Systems. Addison-Wesley, 1980.

name: Hypatia
taglineCn: 古代世界最伟大的女性学者
bio: Hypatia of Alexandria was a philosopher, mathematician, and astronomer who led the Neoplatonic school in Alexandria. She edited mathematical works, built astrolabes and hydrometers, and taught students from across the Mediterranean world. Renowned for her wisdom and eloquence, she was murdered by a Christian mob in 415 CE, making her a symbol of intellectual freedom and the pursuit of knowledge against intolerance.
bioCn: 亚历山大的希帕提娅是一位哲学家、数学家和天文学家，领导着亚历山大的新柏拉图学派。她编辑数学著作、制造星盘和比重计，并教授来自地中海世界各地的学生。她以智慧和雄辩著称，于公元415年被基督教暴徒杀害，成为了知识自由和对抗不容忍的象征。
achievements: Led the Neoplatonic school in Alexandria
Edited works on conic sections and number theory
Built astronomical instruments
achievementsCn: 领导亚历山大新柏拉图学派
编辑圆锥曲线和数论著作
制造天文仪器
references: Watts, Edward J. Hypatia: The Life and Legend of an Ancient Philosopher. Oxford Univ Press, 2017.
Dzielska, Maria. Hypatia of Alexandria. Harvard Univ Press, 1995.

name: Sophie Germain
taglineCn: 自学成才的数学天才
bio: Sophie Germain taught herself mathematics from books in her father's library during the French Revolution, when women were barred from universities. She corresponded with Lagrange and Gauss under a male pseudonym, and her work on Fermat's Last Theorem and elasticity theory earned her the respect of the mathematical community. Gauss later said she proved that women can accomplish remarkable work in the most rigorous sciences.
bioCn: 法国大革命期间，当女性被禁止进入大学时，索菲·热尔曼在父亲的图书馆里自学数学。她以男性笔名与拉格朗日和高斯通信，她在费马大定理和弹性理论方面的工作为她赢得了数学界的尊重。高斯后来说她证明了女性能够在最严谨的科学中取得非凡成就。
achievements: Major contributions to number theory and Fermat's Last Theorem
Pioneered mathematical elasticity theory
Won the French Academy Prize for her work on vibrating surfaces
achievementsCn: 对数论和费马大定理做出重大贡献
开创了数学弹性理论
因振动表面研究获法国科学院奖
references: Laubenbacher, R. & Pengelley, D. 'Voici ce que j'ai trouvé: Sophie Germain's grand plan,' Historia Mathematica, 2010.

name: Emmy Noether
taglineCn: 现代代数之母
bio: Emmy Noether transformed abstract algebra and theoretical physics. Noether's theorem, which proves a deep connection between symmetries and conservation laws, is considered one of the most important theorems in physics. Einstein called her 'the most significant creative mathematical genius thus far produced since the higher education of women began.' Despite her brilliance, she faced persistent gender discrimination and was never granted a full professorship in Germany.
bioCn: 埃米·诺特改变了抽象代数和理论物理。诺特定理证明了对称性和守恒定律之间的深层联系，被认为是物理学中最重要的定理之一。爱因斯坦称她为'自女性高等教育开始以来最重要的创造性数学天才'。尽管才华横溢，她始终面对性别歧视，在德国从未获得正式教授职位。
achievements: Formulated Noether's theorem linking symmetry and conservation
Revolutionized abstract algebra
Called the most important woman in mathematics history
achievementsCn: 提出诺特定理，连接对称性和守恒定律
革新了抽象代数
被称为数学史上最重要的女性
references: Angier, Natalie. 'The Mighty Mathematician You've Never Heard Of.' The New York Times, 2012.

name: Sofia Kovalevskaya
nameCn: 索菲亚·柯瓦列夫斯卡娅
taglineCn: 欧洲首位数学女博士
bio: Sofia Kovalevskaya entered a fictitious marriage to leave Russia and study mathematics in Europe, where women could not enroll independently. She became the first woman in Europe to earn a doctorate in mathematics and the first woman appointed to a full professorship in Northern Europe, at Stockholm University. Her work on partial differential equations and the rotation of rigid bodies remains influential.
bioCn: 索菲亚·柯瓦列夫斯卡娅通过假结婚离开俄国到欧洲学习数学，因为那里的女性不能独立注册入学。她成为欧洲第一位获得数学博士学位的女性，也是北欧第一位被任命为正教授的女性，任教于斯德哥尔摩大学。她在偏微分方程和刚体旋转方面的工作至今仍有影响力。
achievements: First woman in Europe to earn a mathematics PhD
First female full professor in Northern Europe
Major contributions to differential equations and mechanics
achievementsCn: 欧洲第一位获数学博士的女性
北欧第一位女性正教授
对微分方程和力学做出重大贡献
quote: Say what you know, do what you must, come what may.
quoteCn: 说你所知，做你所必须，任凭后果。
references: Koblitz, Ann Hibner. A Convergence of Lives: Sofia Kovalevskaia. Birkhäuser, 1983.

name: Maryam Mirzakhani
tagline: First Woman to Win the Fields Medal
taglineCn: 首位菲尔兹奖女性得主
bio: Maryam Mirzakhani grew up in Tehran and won two gold medals at the International Mathematical Olympiad as a teenager. In 2014, she became the first woman — and the first Iranian — to receive the Fields Medal, mathematics' highest honor, for her groundbreaking work on the dynamics and geometry of Riemann surfaces. She died of breast cancer at age 40, but her legacy continues to inspire mathematicians worldwide.
bioCn: 玛丽亚姆·米尔扎哈尼在德黑兰长大，少年时在国际数学奥林匹克竞赛中获得两枚金牌。2014年，她因在黎曼曲面的动力学和几何学方面的开创性工作，成为第一位——也是第一位伊朗人——获得菲尔兹奖——数学最高荣誉的女性。她40岁因乳腺癌去世，但她的遗产继续激励着全世界的数学家。
achievements: First woman to receive the Fields Medal (2014)
Groundbreaking work on Riemann surfaces
Won two gold medals at International Math Olympiad
achievementsCn: 首位获得菲尔兹奖的女性（2014年）
在黎曼曲面方面的开创性工作
国际数学奥林匹克竞赛两枚金牌
quote: The beauty of mathematics only shows itself to more patient followers.
quoteCn: 数学之美只向更有耐心的追随者展现。
references: Fields Medal citation, International Mathematical Union, 2014.

name: Mary Cartwright
taglineCn: 混沌理论先驱
bio: Mary Cartwright was a British mathematician whose wartime research on radio waves with J.E. Littlewood uncovered some of the first examples of what would later be called chaos theory. She became the first woman to receive the Sylvester Medal, the first woman elected to the Royal Society's council, and the first female president of the London Mathematical Society.
bioCn: 玛丽·卡特赖特是一位英国数学家，她与利特尔伍德在战时对无线电波的研究中发现了后来被称为混沌理论的一些最早的例子。她成为第一位获得西尔维斯特奖章的女性、第一位当选皇家学会理事会成员的女性，以及伦敦数学学会第一位女性主席。
achievements: Discovered early examples of chaos theory
First woman to receive the Sylvester Medal
First female president of the London Mathematical Society
achievementsCn: 发现混沌理论的早期例子
第一位获得西尔维斯特奖章的女性
伦敦数学学会第一位女性主席

name: Maria Gaetana Agnesi
nameCn: 玛利亚·加埃塔纳·阿涅西
tagline: First Woman to Write a Mathematics Textbook
taglineCn: 第一位编写数学教科书的女性
bio: Maria Gaetana Agnesi was an Italian mathematician and philosopher recognized as the first woman to write a mathematics textbook. Her two-volume work Instituzioni analitiche (1748) was one of the first comprehensive treatments of both differential and integral calculus. She was appointed to the chair of mathematics and natural philosophy at the University of Bologna — the first woman to hold such a position at any university.
bioCn: 玛利亚·加埃塔纳·阿涅西是一位意大利数学家和哲学家，被认为是第一位编写数学教科书的女性。她的两卷本著作《分析讲义》（1748年）是最早全面论述微积分的著作之一。她被任命为博洛尼亚大学数学和自然哲学教席——这是任何大学中第一位担任此职位的女性。
achievements: Wrote first comprehensive calculus textbook by a woman
Appointed professor at University of Bologna
Studied the cubic curve known as 'Witch of Agnesi'
achievementsCn: 编写了首部由女性撰写的综合微积分教科书
被任命为博洛尼亚大学教授
研究了被称为'阿涅西的女巫'的三次曲线
references: Mazzotti, Massimo. The World of Maria Gaetana Agnesi. Johns Hopkins Univ Press, 2007.

name: Florence Nightingale
tagline: Pioneer of Statistical Graphics and Data Visualization
taglineCn: 统计图形与数据可视化先驱
bio: While Florence Nightingale is celebrated as the founder of modern nursing, her revolutionary use of statistical analysis and data visualization is equally remarkable. She invented the polar area diagram — a type of pie chart — to dramatically illustrate that most soldiers in the Crimean War died from preventable diseases rather than battle wounds. Her statistical evidence convinced Parliament to reform military healthcare, saving countless lives through the power of data.
bioCn: 虽然弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔作为现代护理学的创始人而闻名，但她在统计分析和数据可视化方面的革命性运用同样引人注目。她发明了极坐标面积图——一种饼图——来戏剧性地说明克里米亚战争中大多数士兵死于可预防的疾病而非战伤。她的统计证据说服议会改革军事医疗保健，通过数据的力量挽救了无数生命。
achievements: Invented the polar area diagram (coxcomb chart)
Pioneered use of statistics in public health policy
First female member of the Royal Statistical Society
achievementsCn: 发明极坐标面积图（鸡冠花图）
开创了统计学在公共卫生政策中的应用
皇家统计学会第一位女性成员
quote: I think one's feelings waste themselves in words; they ought all to be distilled into actions which bring results.
quoteCn: 我认为人的感情浪费在言语中；它们应该全部提炼为带来成果的行动。
references: McDonald, Lynn (ed). The Collected Works of Florence Nightingale. Wilfrid Laurier Univ Press, 2001–2012.
Bostridge, Mark. Florence Nightingale: The Making of an Icon. Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 2008.

name: Wang Zhenyi
taglineCn: 清代天文数学家
bio: Wang Zhenyi was a remarkable Qing Dynasty scholar who mastered mathematics, astronomy, and poetry despite living in an era when women's education was severely limited. She wrote accessible explanations of complex mathematical concepts and correctly explained lunar eclipses through ingenious home experiments using a round table as the globe, a lamp as the sun, and a mirror as the moon. She died at just 29 but left behind a body of work that challenged both scientific and social conventions.
bioCn: 王贞仪是清代一位杰出的学者，尽管生活在女性教育极为有限的时代，她仍精通数学、天文学和诗歌。她为复杂的数学概念撰写了通俗易懂的解释，并通过巧妙的家庭实验——用圆桌代表地球、灯代表太阳、镜子代表月球——正确解释了月食现象。她年仅29岁去世，但留下的著作同时挑战了科学和社会的传统。
achievements: Correctly explained lunar eclipses through home experiments
Made mathematics accessible to wider audiences
Published works on astronomy, mathematics, and poetry
achievementsCn: 通过家庭实验正确解释月食
使数学对更广泛的受众变得易懂
发表天文学、数学和诗歌著作
references: Peterson, Andrea. '4000 Years of Women in Science.'

name: Katherine Johnson
tagline: The Human Computer Behind the Moon Landing
taglineCn: 登月背后的'人体计算机'
bio: Katherine Johnson was a mathematician whose orbital mechanics calculations at NASA were critical to the success of American crewed spaceflight. As an African-American woman working in the segregated South, she broke barriers daily. John Glenn personally requested that she verify the electronic computer's trajectory calculations before his orbital flight. Her story was popularized by the book and film Hidden Figures. She received the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2015 at age 97.
bioCn: 凯瑟琳·约翰逊是一位数学家，她在NASA进行的轨道力学计算对美国载人航天的成功至关重要。作为一位在种族隔离南方工作的非裔美国女性，她每天都在打破壁垒。约翰·格伦亲自要求她在他的轨道飞行前验证电子计算机的轨迹计算。她的故事因书籍和电影《隐藏人物》而广为人知。她在97岁时获得了总统自由勋章。
achievements: Calculated trajectory for first American orbital spaceflight
Verified computer calculations for Apollo 11
Received Presidential Medal of Freedom (2015)
achievementsCn: 计算了美国首次轨道航天飞行的轨迹
为阿波罗11号验证计算机计算
获得总统自由勋章（2015年）
quote: I counted everything. I counted the steps to the road, the steps up to church, the number of dishes and silverware I washed.
quoteCn: 我数一切东西。数走到路上的步数、上教堂的台阶、洗了多少碗碟和银器。
quoteSource: From interviews, documented in NASA oral histories
references: Shetterly, Margot Lee. Hidden Figures. William Morrow, 2016.

name: Marie Curie
tagline: Two-Time Nobel Laureate Who Discovered Radioactivity
taglineCn: 发现放射性的两次诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Marie Skłodowska Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize, the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences, and remains the only person to achieve this distinction. Born in Warsaw, she moved to Paris where she and her husband Pierre discovered polonium and radium. Her research on radioactivity — a term she coined — fundamentally changed physics and chemistry and led to developments in cancer treatment. She died from aplastic anemia, likely caused by prolonged exposure to radiation.
bioCn: 玛丽·斯克沃多夫斯卡·居里是第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女性，也是第一位在两个不同科学领域获得诺贝尔奖的人，至今仍是唯一做到这一点的人。她出生于华沙，搬到巴黎后与丈夫皮埃尔一起发现了钋和镭。她对放射性——这个词正是她创造的——的研究从根本上改变了物理学和化学，并推动了癌症治疗的发展。她因再生障碍性贫血去世，这很可能是长期暴露于辐射造成的。
achievements: First woman to win a Nobel Prize (Physics, 1903)
Only person to win Nobel Prizes in two sciences (Chemistry, 1911)
Discovered polonium and radium
Coined the term 'radioactivity'
achievementsCn: 第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女性（物理学，1903年）
唯一在两个科学领域获诺贝尔奖的人（化学，1911年）
发现钋和镭
创造了'放射性'一词
quote: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood. Now is the time to understand more, so that we may fear less.
quoteCn: 生活中没有什么是值得恐惧的，只有需要理解的。现在是更多理解的时候，以便我们可以少些恐惧。
references: Quinn, Susan. Marie Curie: A Life. Simon & Schuster, 1995.

name: Lise Meitner
tagline: The Physicist Who Explained Nuclear Fission
taglineCn: 解释核裂变的物理学家
bio: Lise Meitner was an Austrian-Swedish physicist who provided the first theoretical explanation of nuclear fission. After fleeing Nazi Germany in 1938, she correctly interpreted the results of experiments by her longtime collaborator Otto Hahn, recognizing that the uranium nucleus had been split. Despite being nominated for the Nobel Prize 49 times, she was excluded from the 1944 Chemistry Nobel — a decision Einstein called 'unjust.' Element 109, meitnerium, is named in her honor.
bioCn: 丽泽·迈特纳是一位奥地利裔瑞典物理学家，她提供了核裂变的第一个理论解释。1938年逃离纳粹德国后，她正确解读了长期合作者奥托·哈恩的实验结果，认识到铀核已被分裂。尽管被诺贝尔奖提名49次，她却被排除在1944年的诺贝尔化学奖之外——爱因斯坦称这一决定'不公正'。第109号元素鿏以她的名字命名。
achievements: Provided first theoretical explanation of nuclear fission
Nominated for Nobel Prize 49 times
Element 109 (meitnerium) named in her honor
achievementsCn: 提供了核裂变的第一个理论解释
被诺贝尔奖提名49次
第109号元素以她的名字命名
quote: Science makes people reach selflessly for truth and objectivity; it teaches people to accept reality, with wonder and admiration.
quoteCn: 科学使人无私地追求真理和客观性；它教人以惊奇和赞叹接受现实。
references: Sime, Ruth Lewin. Lise Meitner: A Life in Physics. Univ of California Press, 1996.

name: Chien-Shiung Wu
taglineCn: 物理学第一夫人
bio: Chien-Shiung Wu conducted the famous Wu experiment in 1957, which proved that parity is not conserved in weak nuclear interactions — overturning a fundamental assumption of physics. While her male colleagues Tsung-Dao Lee and Chen-Ning Yang received the Nobel Prize for the theoretical prediction, Wu's experimental proof was excluded from the award. Known as the 'First Lady of Physics' and the 'Chinese Marie Curie,' she received the inaugural Wolf Prize in Physics and was honored with a U.S. postage stamp.
bioCn: 吴健雄在1957年进行了著名的吴氏实验，证明了在弱核相互作用中宇称不守恒——推翻了物理学的一个基本假设。虽然她的男性同事李政道和杨振宁因理论预测获得了诺贝尔奖，但吴健雄的实验证明却被排除在奖项之外。她被称为'物理学第一夫人'和'中国的居里夫人'，获得了首届沃尔夫物理学奖，并被印在美国邮票上。
achievements: Proved parity non-conservation (Wu experiment)
Received inaugural Wolf Prize in Physics
Honored with U.S. postage stamp (2021)
achievementsCn: 证明宇称不守恒（吴氏实验）
获得首届沃尔夫物理学奖
被印在美国邮票上（2021年）
quote: There is only one thing worse than coming home from the lab to a sink full of dirty dishes. And that is not going to the lab at all!
quoteCn: 唯一比从实验室回家面对一水池脏碗碟更糟的事，就是根本没去实验室！
references: Chiang, Tsai-Chien. Madame Wu Chien-Shiung: The First Lady of Physics Research. World Scientific, 2014.

name: Maria Goeppert Mayer
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Nuclear Shell Model
taglineCn: 核壳层模型诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Maria Goeppert Mayer was a theoretical physicist who proposed the nuclear shell model of the atomic nucleus, for which she received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1963 — only the second woman to do so after Marie Curie. For much of her career, anti-nepotism rules prevented her from holding paid positions at universities where her husband worked, forcing her to pursue research as a volunteer or in unpaid positions.
bioCn: 玛丽亚·格佩特-梅耶是一位理论物理学家，她提出了原子核的核壳层模型，并因此获得1963年诺贝尔物理学奖——继居里夫人之后第二位获此殊荣的女性。在她职业生涯的大部分时间里，反裙带关系规定阻止她在丈夫工作的大学担任有薪职位，迫使她以志愿者或无薪职位进行研究。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (1963) for nuclear shell model
Second woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Pioneered two-photon absorption theory
achievementsCn: 因核壳层模型获诺贝尔物理学奖（1963年）
第二位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
开创两光子吸收理论

name: Émilie du Châtelet
taglineCn: 将牛顿《原理》翻译成法语
bio: Émilie du Châtelet was a French natural philosopher and mathematician who made Newton's work accessible to the European continent. Her translation and commentary on Newton's Principia Mathematica, published posthumously, remains the standard French translation to this day. She also proposed that kinetic energy is proportional to the square of velocity — a concept later confirmed and foundational to modern physics. She died at 42 from complications of childbirth, days after completing her translation.
bioCn: 埃米莉·杜·夏特莱是一位法国自然哲学家和数学家，她使牛顿的著作在欧洲大陆得到传播。她对牛顿《自然哲学的数学原理》的翻译和评注在她去世后出版，至今仍是标准的法语译本。她还提出动能与速度的平方成正比——这一概念后来得到证实，成为现代物理学的基础。她在完成翻译后数天，因分娩并发症去世，年仅42岁。
achievements: Translated Newton's Principia Mathematica into French
Proposed kinetic energy proportional to velocity squared
Her translation remains the French standard after 275 years
achievementsCn: 将牛顿《自然哲学的数学原理》翻译成法语
提出动能与速度平方成正比
她的译本在275年后仍是法语标准版
references: Zinsser, Judith. Emilie Du Chatelet: Daring Genius of the Enlightenment. Penguin, 2007.

name: Donna Strickland
taglineCn: 激光物理诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Donna Strickland shared the 2018 Nobel Prize in Physics for developing chirped pulse amplification (CPA), a technique that generates ultrashort, high-intensity laser pulses now used in millions of corrective eye surgeries annually. She was only the third woman in history to receive the Nobel Prize in Physics. At the time of her award, she was an associate professor — she did not yet have a Wikipedia page, highlighting the persistent invisibility of women in science.
bioCn: 唐娜·斯特里克兰因开发啁啾脉冲放大（CPA）技术而分享了2018年诺贝尔物理学奖，该技术产生超短、高强度激光脉冲，现在每年用于数百万次矫正眼科手术。她是历史上第三位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性。获奖时她还是副教授——甚至还没有维基百科页面，这凸显了女性在科学中的持续隐形。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (2018) for chirped pulse amplification
Third woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Technology enables millions of eye surgeries annually
achievementsCn: 因啁啾脉冲放大获诺贝尔物理学奖（2018年）
第三位获诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
技术每年支持数百万次眼科手术
quote: We need to celebrate women physicists because they're out there.
quoteCn: 我们需要赞美女性物理学家，因为她们就在那里。

name: Jocelyn Bell Burnell
taglineCn: 脉冲星的发现者
bio: As a graduate student in 1967, Jocelyn Bell Burnell discovered pulsars — rapidly rotating neutron stars that emit regular pulses of radio waves. The 1974 Nobel Prize for this discovery was controversially awarded to her supervisor Antony Hewish and Martin Ryle, excluding Bell Burnell. Despite this injustice, she has had an extraordinary career in astrophysics and donated the entire £2.3 million from her 2018 Breakthrough Prize to fund scholarships for underrepresented physics students.
bioCn: 1967年，作为研究生的乔斯林·贝尔·伯奈尔发现了脉冲星——快速旋转的中子星，发射规则的射电脉冲。1974年诺贝尔奖因这一发现颁发给了她的导师安东尼·休伊什和马丁·赖尔，争议性地将贝尔·伯奈尔排除在外。尽管遭受了这种不公正，她在天体物理学方面取得了非凡的职业成就，并将2018年突破奖的全部230万英镑捐赠用于资助代表性不足的物理学生奖学金。
achievements: Discovered pulsars as a graduate student (1967)
Donated £2.3M Breakthrough Prize to student scholarships
Served as president of the Royal Astronomical Society
achievementsCn: 作为研究生发现脉冲星（1967年）
将230万英镑突破奖全部捐赠给学生奖学金
担任皇家天文学会主席
quote: I feel I've done very well out of not getting a Nobel Prize.
quoteCn: 我觉得没获得诺贝尔奖让我的人生过得很好。
references: Burnell, J.B. 'Little Green Men, White Dwarfs, or Pulsars?' Cosmic Search, 1977.

name: Vera Rubin
tagline: Proved the Existence of Dark Matter
taglineCn: 证明了暗物质的存在
bio: Vera Rubin's observations of galaxy rotation curves provided the first strong evidence that most of the matter in the universe is invisible dark matter. Her meticulous work in the 1970s showed that galaxies rotate far faster than expected, implying the existence of vast amounts of unseen mass. Despite revolutionizing our understanding of the cosmos, she was never awarded the Nobel Prize. In 2020, the Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile was named in her honor.
bioCn: 维拉·鲁宾对星系旋转曲线的观测提供了第一个有力证据，表明宇宙中的大部分物质是不可见的暗物质。她在20世纪70年代的细致工作表明，星系的旋转速度远超预期，暗示存在大量看不见的质量。尽管她彻底改变了我们对宇宙的理解，却从未获得诺贝尔奖。2020年，智利的维拉·C·鲁宾天文台以她的名字命名。
achievements: Provided first strong evidence for dark matter
Vera C. Rubin Observatory named in her honor
Received National Medal of Science
achievementsCn: 提供了暗物质的第一个有力证据
维拉·C·鲁宾天文台以她命名
获得国家科学奖章
quote: Science progresses best when observations force us to alter our preconceptions.
quoteCn: 当观测迫使我们改变先入之见时，科学进步最大。
references: Rubin, Vera. Bright Galaxies, Dark Matters. AIP Press, 1997.

name: Cecilia Payne-Gaposchkin
nameCn: 塞西莉亚·佩恩-加波施金
tagline: Discovered What Stars Are Made Of
taglineCn: 发现恒星由什么组成
bio: Cecilia Payne-Gaposchkin's 1925 doctoral thesis was described by astronomer Otto Struve as 'the most brilliant PhD thesis ever written in astronomy.' She demonstrated that stars are composed primarily of hydrogen and helium, contradicting the prevailing belief that they had the same composition as Earth. Initially dissuaded from publishing this conclusion by Henry Norris Russell, her findings were later confirmed and are now fundamental to astrophysics.
bioCn: 塞西莉亚·佩恩-加波施金1925年的博士论文被天文学家奥托·斯特鲁维描述为'天文学史上写得最出色的博士论文'。她证明恒星主要由氢和氦组成，推翻了当时认为恒星与地球成分相同的主流观点。最初她被亨利·诺里斯·罗素劝阻不要发表这一结论，但她的发现后来得到证实，现在已成为天体物理学的基础。
achievements: Discovered stars are primarily hydrogen and helium
Wrote what is called the most brilliant astronomy PhD thesis
First woman to become a full professor at Harvard
achievementsCn: 发现恒星主要由氢和氦组成
撰写了被称为最出色的天文学博士论文
哈佛大学第一位女性正教授
quote: The reward of the young scientist is the emotional thrill of being the first person in the history of the world to see something or to understand something.
quoteCn: 年轻科学家的回报，是成为世界历史上第一个看到或理解某事的人所带来的情感激动。
references: Payne-Gaposchkin, C. An Autobiography and Other Recollections. Cambridge Univ Press, 1984.

name: Hertha Ayrton
taglineCn: 物理学家与电气工程师
bio: Hertha Ayrton was the first woman to read a paper before the Royal Society of London and the first woman to win the Hughes Medal for original scientific research. Her work on electric arcs and ripple marks in sand and water had both theoretical and practical significance. She held 26 patents and was a passionate suffragette. When asked whether she minded being called a woman scientist, she replied that she did not understand the distinction.
bioCn: 赫莎·艾尔顿是第一位在伦敦皇家学会宣读论文的女性，也是第一位因原创科学研究获得休斯奖章的女性。她在电弧以及沙和水中的涟漪方面的工作具有理论和实践双重意义。她拥有26项专利，并且是一位热情的女性参政权运动者。当被问及是否介意被称为女科学家时，她回答说她不理解这种区分。
achievements: First woman to read a paper before the Royal Society
First woman to win the Hughes Medal
Held 26 patents
achievementsCn: 第一位在皇家学会宣读论文的女性
第一位获得休斯奖章的女性
拥有26项专利
references: Sharp, Evelyn. Hertha Ayrton: A Memoir. Edward Arnold, 1926.

name: Andrea Ghez
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Supermassive Black Hole Discovery
taglineCn: 因超大质量黑洞发现获诺贝尔奖
bio: Andrea Ghez shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Physics for her discovery of a supermassive compact object at the center of the Milky Way galaxy. Using the Keck Observatory in Hawaii, she developed techniques to peer through interstellar dust and tracked stars orbiting the invisible object now known as Sagittarius A*. She became the fourth woman to win the Nobel Prize in Physics.
bioCn: 安德里亚·盖兹因发现银河系中心的超大质量致密天体而分享了2020年诺贝尔物理学奖。利用夏威夷的凯克天文台，她开发了穿透星际尘埃的技术，追踪了围绕现在称为人马座A*的不可见天体运行的恒星。她成为第四位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (2020) for black hole discovery
Fourth woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Pioneered adaptive optics techniques in astronomy
achievementsCn: 因黑洞发现获诺贝尔物理学奖（2020年）
第四位获诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
开创天文学自适应光学技术

name: Mildred Dresselhaus
nameCn: 米尔德里德·德雷塞尔豪斯
taglineCn: 碳科学女王
bio: Mildred Dresselhaus, known as the 'Queen of Carbon Science,' was an MIT physicist whose research on carbon nanostructures laid the groundwork for the field of nanotechnology. Her work on graphite, carbon nanotubes, and thermoelectric materials helped make possible technologies from lightweight composites to energy-harvesting devices. She received the Presidential Medal of Freedom and was the first woman to win the National Medal of Science in Engineering.
bioCn: 米尔德里德·德雷塞尔豪斯被称为'碳科学女王'，是MIT物理学家，她对碳纳米结构的研究为纳米技术领域奠定了基础。她在石墨、碳纳米管和热电材料方面的工作使从轻质复合材料到能量收集设备的技术成为可能。她获得了总统自由勋章，是第一位获得国家工程科学奖章的女性。
achievements: Pioneered carbon nanostructure research
Presidential Medal of Freedom recipient
First woman to win National Medal of Science in Engineering
achievementsCn: 开创碳纳米结构研究
获得总统自由勋章
第一位获得国家工程科学奖章的女性

name: Rosalind Franklin
tagline: The Woman Behind DNA's Double Helix
taglineCn: DNA双螺旋背后的女性
bio: Rosalind Franklin's X-ray diffraction image known as Photo 51 was the crucial evidence that revealed the double helix structure of DNA. Her colleague Maurice Wilkins showed the photograph to James Watson without her knowledge, and Watson and Crick used it to build their famous model. Franklin never knew her data had been shared. She died of ovarian cancer at 37, four years before Watson, Crick, and Wilkins received the Nobel Prize — which is not awarded posthumously.
bioCn: 罗莎琳德·富兰克林的X射线衍射图像——著名的Photo 51——是揭示DNA双螺旋结构的关键证据。她的同事莫里斯·威尔金斯在她不知情的情况下将照片展示给了詹姆斯·沃森，沃森和克里克利用它构建了他们著名的模型。富兰克林从不知道她的数据被分享了。她37岁死于卵巢癌，在沃森、克里克和威尔金斯获得诺贝尔奖的四年前——该奖不授予已故者。
achievements: Captured Photo 51, key evidence for DNA's structure
Pioneered X-ray diffraction studies of biological molecules
Also made major contributions to understanding viruses
achievementsCn: 拍摄Photo 51，DNA结构的关键证据
开创了生物分子的X射线衍射研究
对理解病毒也做出了重大贡献
quote: Science and everyday life cannot and should not be separated.
quoteCn: 科学与日常生活不能也不应该分离。
references: Maddox, Brenda. Rosalind Franklin: The Dark Lady of DNA. HarperCollins, 2002.

name: Dorothy Hodgkin
taglineCn: X射线晶体学诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Dorothy Crowfoot Hodgkin advanced the technique of X-ray crystallography to determine the three-dimensional structures of biomolecules. She confirmed the structure of penicillin, mapped vitamin B12, and later determined the structure of insulin — work that took her 35 years. She received the 1964 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, becoming only the third woman to receive the prize. She was also a lifelong peace activist.
bioCn: 多萝西·克劳福特·霍奇金推进了X射线晶体学技术，用以确定生物分子的三维结构。她确认了青霉素的结构、绘制了维生素B12的图谱，后来还确定了胰岛素的结构——这项工作耗费了她35年。她获得了1964年诺贝尔化学奖，成为第三位获此殊荣的女性。她也是一位终身和平活动家。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1964)
Determined structures of penicillin, vitamin B12, and insulin
Only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in science
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔化学奖（1964年）
确定了青霉素、维生素B12和胰岛素的结构
唯一获得诺贝尔科学奖的英国女性
quote: I was captured for life by chemistry and by crystals.
quoteCn: 化学和晶体俘获了我的一生。
references: Ferry, Georgina. Dorothy Hodgkin: A Life. Granta Books, 1998.

name: Alice Ball
tagline: Developed the First Effective Leprosy Treatment
taglineCn: 开发了第一种有效的麻风病治疗方法
bio: Alice Ball was an African-American chemist who developed the first effective treatment for leprosy (Hansen's disease) at the age of 23. Her 'Ball Method' made chaulmoogra oil injectable by isolating its active compounds, treating thousands of patients worldwide. She died at just 24 from chlorine poisoning in a laboratory accident. Her work was initially claimed by a colleague, but her contributions were formally recognized by the University of Hawaii in 2000.
bioCn: 爱丽丝·鲍尔是一位非裔美国化学家，23岁时开发了第一种有效的麻风病（汉森病）治疗方法。她的'鲍尔方法'通过分离活性化合物使大风子油可注射，在全球治疗了数千名患者。她年仅24岁因实验室氯气中毒去世。她的工作最初被一位同事占为己有，但2000年夏威夷大学正式承认了她的贡献。
achievements: Developed the Ball Method for treating leprosy
Youngest and first African-American master's graduate at University of Hawaii
Treated thousands of leprosy patients worldwide
achievementsCn: 开发了治疗麻风病的鲍尔方法
夏威夷大学最年轻的、也是第一位非裔美国硕士毕业生
在全球治疗了数千名麻风病患者
references: Swaby, Rachel. Headstrong: 52 Women Who Changed Science. Crown/Broadway, 2015.

name: Irène Joliot-Curie
taglineCn: 人工放射性诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Irène Joliot-Curie, daughter of Marie and Pierre Curie, shared the 1935 Nobel Prize in Chemistry with her husband Frédéric for their discovery of artificial radioactivity. She demonstrated that radioactive elements could be artificially produced, opening the door to nuclear medicine and advancing nuclear physics. Like her mother, she died from leukemia caused by prolonged radiation exposure. She also served in the French government as undersecretary of state for scientific research.
bioCn: 伊雷娜·约里奥-居里是玛丽和皮埃尔·居里的女儿，与丈夫弗雷德里克因发现人工放射性而分享了1935年诺贝尔化学奖。她证明放射性元素可以人工制造，为核医学打开了大门，推动了核物理学的发展。和她的母亲一样，她死于长期辐射暴露引起的白血病。她还在法国政府担任科学研究国务副秘书。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1935) for artificial radioactivity
Part of the only mother-daughter Nobel Prize pair
Served as French undersecretary of state for scientific research
achievementsCn: 因人工放射性获诺贝尔化学奖（1935年）
唯一的母女诺贝尔奖得主之一
担任法国科学研究国务副秘书

name: Gertrude Elion
taglineCn: 革新了药物开发
bio: Gertrude Elion developed a revolutionary approach to drug design that used differences in biochemistry between normal cells and pathogens. Her method led to treatments for leukemia, herpes, malaria, gout, and organ transplant rejection. She shared the 1988 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine. Despite never completing a PhD — she was denied admission to graduate programs because she was a woman — her innovations saved millions of lives.
bioCn: 格特鲁德·埃利恩开发了一种革命性的药物设计方法，利用正常细胞和病原体之间的生化差异。她的方法导致了白血病、疱疹、疟疾、痛风和器官移植排斥反应的治疗方案。她分享了1988年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。尽管从未完成博士学位——她因为是女性而被拒绝进入研究生项目——她的创新挽救了数百万人的生命。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1988)
Developed treatments for leukemia, herpes, and malaria
Revolutionized rational drug design methodology
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（1988年）
开发了白血病、疱疹和疟疾的治疗方法
革新了理性药物设计方法论

name: Jennifer Doudna
taglineCn: CRISPR基因编辑的共同发明者
bio: Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for developing CRISPR-Cas9, a revolutionary tool for editing DNA with unprecedented precision. Their discovery has transformed biology, enabling potential cures for genetic diseases, creation of disease-resistant crops, and new approaches to treating cancer. It is one of the most consequential scientific discoveries of the 21st century.
bioCn: 珍妮弗·杜德纳和埃曼纽尔·夏彭蒂耶因开发CRISPR-Cas9——一种以前所未有的精度编辑DNA的革命性工具——而分享了2020年诺贝尔化学奖。她们的发现改变了生物学，使遗传疾病的潜在治愈、抗病作物的创造和治疗癌症的新方法成为可能。这是21世纪最具深远影响的科学发现之一。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2020) for CRISPR-Cas9
Revolutionized genome editing
Founded the Innovative Genomics Institute
achievementsCn: 因CRISPR-Cas9获诺贝尔化学奖（2020年）
革新了基因组编辑
创立创新基因组学研究所
quote: The power to control our species' genetic future is awesome and terrifying.
quoteCn: 控制我们物种基因未来的力量既令人敬畏又令人恐惧。
references: Doudna, J. & Sternberg, S. A Crack in Creation. Houghton Mifflin, 2017.

name: Emmanuelle Charpentier
taglineCn: CRISPR基因编辑的共同发明者
bio: Emmanuelle Charpentier is a French microbiologist and biochemist who co-developed CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology with Jennifer Doudna. Her discovery of tracrRNA, a molecule essential for bacterial immune defense, was the key insight that led to the development of the CRISPR tool. She shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, making Doudna and Charpentier the first all-female team to share a science Nobel.
bioCn: 埃曼纽尔·夏彭蒂耶是一位法国微生物学家和生物化学家，与珍妮弗·杜德纳共同开发了CRISPR-Cas9基因编辑技术。她对tracrRNA——一种对细菌免疫防御至关重要的分子——的发现是开发CRISPR工具的关键洞见。她分享了2020年诺贝尔化学奖，使杜德纳和夏彭蒂耶成为第一个分享诺贝尔科学奖的全女性团队。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Chemistry (2020) for CRISPR-Cas9
Discovered tracrRNA crucial for CRISPR mechanism
First all-female team to share a science Nobel Prize
achievementsCn: 因CRISPR-Cas9获诺贝尔化学奖（2020年）
发现对CRISPR机制至关重要的tracrRNA
第一个分享诺贝尔科学奖的全女性团队

name: Tu Youyou
taglineCn: 发现青蒿素治疗疟疾
bio: Tu Youyou discovered artemisinin, a compound derived from traditional Chinese medicine that has saved millions of lives from malaria. During the Cultural Revolution, she led a secret military project that screened thousands of herbal remedies. Inspired by a 1,600-year-old text by Ge Hong, she developed a low-temperature extraction method that preserved the compound's efficacy. She received the 2015 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine — the first Chinese Nobel laureate in the sciences and the first without a doctoral degree or overseas study experience.
bioCn: 屠呦呦发现了青蒿素——一种源自中国传统医学的化合物，已经拯救了数百万人免于疟疾。在文化大革命期间，她领导了一个秘密军事项目，筛选了数千种草药。受葛洪1600年前古籍的启发，她开发了保持化合物功效的低温提取方法。她获得了2015年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖——中国第一位科学领域的诺贝尔奖得主，也是第一位没有博士学位和海外留学经历的获奖者。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2015)
Discovered artemisinin, saving millions from malaria
First Chinese scientist to win a Nobel Prize
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2015年）
发现青蒿素，拯救数百万人免于疟疾
中国第一位获得诺贝尔奖的科学家
quote: Every scientist dreams of doing something that can help the world.
quoteCn: 每个科学家都梦想做一些能帮助世界的事情。

name: Elizabeth Blackwell
tagline: First Woman to Earn a Medical Degree in the U.S.
taglineCn: 美国第一位获得医学学位的女性
bio: Elizabeth Blackwell became the first woman to receive a medical degree in the United States in 1849, graduating first in her class from Geneva Medical College. She faced enormous prejudice — her acceptance had been put to a student vote as a joke, but she persevered. She went on to found the New York Infirmary for Indigent Women and Children and established a medical college for women, opening the door for generations of female physicians.
bioCn: 伊丽莎白·布莱克韦尔于1849年成为美国第一位获得医学学位的女性，以全班第一名从日内瓦医学院毕业。她面对了巨大的偏见——她的录取曾被作为玩笑交由学生投票，但她坚持了下来。她后来创办了纽约贫困妇女儿童医院，并建立了女性医学院，为几代女性医生打开了大门。
achievements: First woman to earn a medical degree in the U.S. (1849)
Founded New York Infirmary for Indigent Women and Children
Established a medical college for women
achievementsCn: 美国第一位获得医学学位的女性（1849年）
创办纽约贫困妇女儿童医院
建立女性医学院
quote: It is not easy to be a pioneer — but oh, it is fascinating!
quoteCn: 成为先驱并不容易——但天哪，这太迷人了！
references: Nimura, Janice. The Doctors Blackwell. W.W. Norton, 2021.

name: Barbara McClintock
taglineCn: 遗传转座的发现者
bio: Barbara McClintock discovered genetic transposition — the ability of genes to change position on chromosomes — decades before the scientific community accepted her findings. Working alone with maize plants at Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory, she showed that genes could move and regulate the expression of other genes. Her work, initially dismissed, was vindicated when she received the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1983, the first woman to win the prize unshared.
bioCn: 芭芭拉·麦克林托克发现了遗传转座——基因在染色体上改变位置的能力——比科学界接受她的发现早了几十年。她在冷泉港实验室独自研究玉米植物，表明基因可以移动并调节其他基因的表达。她的工作最初被否定，但在1983年获得诺贝尔生理学或医学奖时得到了平反，她是第一位独享该奖的女性。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1983)
Discovered genetic transposition (jumping genes)
First woman to win an unshared Nobel in Physiology or Medicine
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（1983年）
发现遗传转座（跳跃基因）
第一位独享诺贝尔生理学或医学奖的女性
quote: If you know you are on the right track, if you have this inner knowledge, then nobody can turn you off.
quoteCn: 如果你知道自己走在正确的道路上，如果你有这种内在的认知，那么没有人能阻止你。
quoteSource: Documented in Keller, Evelyn Fox. A Feeling for the Organism, 1983.
references: Keller, Evelyn Fox. A Feeling for the Organism. W.H. Freeman, 1983.

name: Rachel Carson
taglineCn: 环保运动之母
bio: Rachel Carson was a marine biologist and author whose 1962 book Silent Spring documented the devastating effects of pesticides on the environment, particularly DDT's impact on bird populations. Despite a vicious industry campaign to discredit her, her meticulous scientific research catalyzed the modern environmental movement and led directly to the creation of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and the ban on DDT.
bioCn: 蕾切尔·卡逊是一位海洋生物学家和作家，她1962年的著作《寂静的春天》记录了农药对环境的毁灭性影响，特别是DDT对鸟类种群的影响。尽管面对行业恶意的诋毁运动，她细致的科学研究催化了现代环保运动，直接促成了美国环境保护署的创建和DDT的禁用。
achievements: Published Silent Spring, catalyzing the environmental movement
Led to creation of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency
Time magazine's 100 most influential people of the 20th century
achievementsCn: 出版《寂静的春天》，催化环保运动
促成美国环境保护署的创建
《时代》杂志20世纪100位最具影响力人物
quote: The more clearly we can focus our attention on the wonders and realities of the universe about us, the less taste we shall have for destruction.
quoteCn: 我们越清楚地将注意力集中在周围宇宙的奇迹和现实上，就越不会有毁灭的欲望。
references: Carson, Rachel. Silent Spring. Houghton Mifflin, 1962.
Lear, Linda. Rachel Carson: Witness for Nature. Henry Holt, 1997.

name: Jane Goodall
taglineCn: 世界最杰出的黑猩猩专家
bio: Jane Goodall revolutionized our understanding of primates through her 60-year study of chimpanzees in Gombe, Tanzania. She was the first to observe that chimpanzees make and use tools — a discovery that blurred the line between humans and other animals. Without a college degree when she began, she earned her PhD from Cambridge and became one of the most influential scientists and conservationists in history.
bioCn: 珍·古道尔通过在坦桑尼亚贡贝长达60年的黑猩猩研究，彻底改变了我们对灵长类动物的理解。她是第一个观察到黑猩猩制造和使用工具的人——这一发现模糊了人类和其他动物之间的界限。开始研究时她没有大学学位，后来在剑桥获得博士学位，成为历史上最具影响力的科学家和保育人士之一。
achievements: Discovered chimpanzees make and use tools
60+ year study of chimpanzees in Gombe, Tanzania
UN Messenger of Peace
achievementsCn: 发现黑猩猩制造和使用工具
在坦桑尼亚贡贝进行60多年的黑猩猩研究
联合国和平信使
quote: What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make.
quoteCn: 你所做的事会产生影响，你必须决定你想产生什么样的影响。
references: Goodall, Jane. In the Shadow of Man. Houghton Mifflin, 1971.

name: Mary Anning
taglineCn: 古生物学先驱
bio: Mary Anning was a fossil collector and self-taught paleontologist who made some of the most important finds in the history of geology. Working the treacherous cliffs of Lyme Regis, she discovered the first correctly identified ichthyosaur skeleton at age 12, and later found the first plesiosaur and the first pterosaur known outside Germany. Despite her extraordinary discoveries, as a working-class woman she was excluded from the scientific societies that studied her finds.
bioCn: 玛丽·安宁是一位化石收集者和自学成才的古生物学家，她做出了地质学史上一些最重要的发现。在莱姆里吉斯危险的悬崖上工作，她12岁时发现了第一具被正确鉴定的鱼龙骨骼，后来又发现了第一具蛇颈龙和德国以外已知的第一只翼龙。尽管她的发现非同寻常，但作为一名工人阶级女性，她被排斥在研究她发现的科学学会之外。
achievements: Discovered first correctly identified ichthyosaur skeleton
Found first plesiosaur and first British pterosaur
Fundamental contributions to understanding prehistoric life
achievementsCn: 发现第一具被正确鉴定的鱼龙骨骼
发现第一具蛇颈龙和第一只英国翼龙
对理解史前生命做出根本性贡献
references: Emling, Shelley. The Fossil Hunter. Palgrave Macmillan, 2009.

name: Rita Levi-Montalcini
tagline: Nobel Laureate Who Discovered Nerve Growth Factor
taglineCn: 发现神经生长因子的诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Rita Levi-Montalcini discovered nerve growth factor (NGF), revealing how cells grow and develop. Banned from university by Mussolini's racial laws as a Jewish Italian, she set up a laboratory in her bedroom and continued her research in hiding during World War II. She shared the 1986 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine and remained scientifically active until her death at 103, making her the longest-lived Nobel laureate at that time.
bioCn: 丽塔·列维-蒙塔尔奇尼发现了神经生长因子（NGF），揭示了细胞如何生长和发育。作为意大利犹太人，她因墨索里尼的种族法被禁止进入大学，于是在卧室里建了一个实验室，在二战期间秘密继续研究。她分享了1986年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖，并一直从事科学工作直到103岁去世，是当时最长寿的诺贝尔奖得主。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1986)
Discovered nerve growth factor (NGF)
Continued research hidden from fascist authorities
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（1986年）
发现神经生长因子（NGF）
在法西斯当局眼皮底下秘密继续研究
quote: Above all, don't fear difficult moments. The best comes from them.
quoteCn: 最重要的是，不要害怕困难时刻。最好的东西正是从中而来。

name: Nettie Stevens
taglineCn: 发现性别的染色体基础
bio: Nettie Stevens discovered that sex is determined by chromosomes — specifically that XX chromosomes produce females and XY produce males in many species. Working with mealworms, she provided the crucial evidence linking chromosomes to inherited traits. Her colleague Edmund Beecher Wilson published similar findings simultaneously but received most of the credit, while Stevens' contribution was largely forgotten for decades.
bioCn: 内蒂·史蒂文斯发现性别是由染色体决定的——特别是在许多物种中XX染色体产生雌性，XY产生雄性。通过研究黄粉虫，她提供了将染色体与遗传特征联系起来的关键证据。她的同事埃德蒙·比彻·威尔逊同时发表了类似发现但获得了大部分荣誉，而史蒂文斯的贡献在几十年里几乎被遗忘。
achievements: Discovered chromosomal basis of sex determination
Linked chromosomes to inherited traits
Pioneered the field of cytogenetics
achievementsCn: 发现性别决定的染色体基础
将染色体与遗传特征联系起来
开创了细胞遗传学领域
references: Brush, Stephen G. 'Nettie M. Stevens and the Discovery of Sex Determination by Chromosomes,' Isis, 1978.

name: Françoise Barré-Sinoussi
nameCn: 弗朗索瓦丝·巴尔-西诺西
taglineCn: HIV的共同发现者
bio: Françoise Barré-Sinoussi co-discovered the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) as the cause of AIDS in 1983 at the Pasteur Institute in Paris. Her identification of the retrovirus was crucial for developing diagnostic tests and treatments that have saved millions of lives. She received the 2008 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine and has dedicated her career to fighting HIV/AIDS, particularly in developing countries.
bioCn: 弗朗索瓦丝·巴尔-西诺西于1983年在巴黎巴斯德研究所共同发现了人类免疫缺陷病毒（HIV）是艾滋病的病因。她对逆转录病毒的鉴定对于开发诊断测试和治疗方法至关重要，挽救了数百万人的生命。她获得了2008年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖，并将一生致力于抗击HIV/AIDS，特别是在发展中国家。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2008)
Co-discovered HIV as cause of AIDS
Dedicated career to fighting HIV/AIDS globally
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2008年）
共同发现HIV是艾滋病的病因
毕生致力于在全球范围内抗击HIV/AIDS

name: Christiane Nüsslein-Volhard
nameCn: 克里斯蒂安娜·纽斯林-沃尔哈德
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Embryonic Development Research
taglineCn: 胚胎发育研究诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Christiane Nüsslein-Volhard shared the 1995 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for her discoveries concerning the genetic control of early embryonic development. Using fruit flies as a model organism, she identified the genes that control the body plan of the developing embryo, showing how a fertilized egg becomes a complex organism. She later established a foundation to support young women scientists with children.
bioCn: 克里斯蒂安娜·纽斯林-沃尔哈德因发现早期胚胎发育的遗传控制机制而分享了1995年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。以果蝇作为模式生物，她鉴定了控制发育中胚胎体型的基因，展示了受精卵如何变成复杂有机体。她后来建立了一个基金会来支持有孩子的年轻女科学家。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1995)
Identified genes controlling embryonic body plan
Founded foundation supporting women scientists with children
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（1995年）
鉴定控制胚胎体型的基因
创建支持有孩子的女科学家的基金会

name: Linda Buck
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Discovering Olfactory Receptors
taglineCn: 发现嗅觉受体的诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Linda Buck shared the 2004 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for her discoveries of odorant receptors and the organization of the olfactory system. She identified approximately 1,000 different olfactory receptor genes, revealing how humans can detect and distinguish about 10,000 different odors. Her work opened entirely new areas of neuroscience research.
bioCn: 琳达·巴克因发现气味受体和嗅觉系统的组织方式而分享了2004年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。她鉴定了约1000个不同的嗅觉受体基因，揭示了人类如何检测和区分约10000种不同的气味。她的工作开辟了全新的神经科学研究领域。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2004)
Identified ~1,000 olfactory receptor genes
Revealed how humans detect 10,000 different odors
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2004年）
鉴定了约1000个嗅觉受体基因
揭示了人类如何检测10000种不同气味

name: Elizabeth Garrett Anderson
nameCn: 伊丽莎白·加勒特·安德森
taglineCn: 英国第一位女医师
bio: Elizabeth Garrett Anderson was the first woman to qualify as a physician and surgeon in Britain. Rejected by every medical school, she qualified through the Society of Apothecaries, which then changed its rules to prevent other women from following. She co-founded the London School of Medicine for Women and became the first female mayor in England. Her persistence paved the way for women in British medicine.
bioCn: 伊丽莎白·加勒特·安德森是英国第一位获得医师和外科医生资格的女性。被每所医学院拒绝后，她通过药剂师协会获得资格，该协会随后更改了规则以阻止其他女性效仿。她共同创办了伦敦女子医学院，并成为英格兰第一位女性市长。她的坚持为英国医学界的女性铺平了道路。
achievements: First woman to qualify as physician in Britain
Co-founded London School of Medicine for Women
First female mayor in England
achievementsCn: 英国第一位获得医师资格的女性
共同创办伦敦女子医学院
英格兰第一位女性市长
references: Manton, Jo. Elizabeth Garrett Anderson. Methuen, 1965.

name: Virginia Apgar
tagline: Creator of the Apgar Score for Newborns
taglineCn: 新生儿阿普加评分系统创立者
bio: Virginia Apgar developed the Apgar Score in 1952, a simple and rapid method to assess the health of newborn babies immediately after birth. This five-criteria scoring system — Appearance, Pulse, Grimace, Activity, Respiration — has been used on virtually every baby born in a modern hospital and is credited with dramatically reducing infant mortality worldwide. She was also the first woman to become a full professor at Columbia University's medical school.
bioCn: 弗吉尼亚·阿普加于1952年开发了阿普加评分，一种在婴儿出生后立即快速评估新生儿健康状况的简单方法。这个五项标准评分系统——外观、脉搏、反射、活动、呼吸——几乎在现代医院出生的每个婴儿身上使用，被认为大幅降低了全球婴儿死亡率。她也是哥伦比亚大学医学院第一位女性正教授。
achievements: Created the Apgar Score used worldwide on newborns
Dramatically reduced infant mortality globally
First female full professor at Columbia medical school
achievementsCn: 创建了全球使用的新生儿阿普加评分
大幅降低了全球婴儿死亡率
哥伦比亚大学医学院第一位女性正教授
quote: Nobody, but nobody, is going to stop breathing on me.
quoteCn: 没有人，任何人都不能在我面前停止呼吸。
references: Apgar, V. 'A Proposal for a New Method of Evaluation of the Newborn Infant,' Anesthesia & Analgesia, 1953.

name: Caroline Herschel
tagline: First Woman to Discover a Comet
taglineCn: 第一位发现彗星的女性
bio: Caroline Herschel was the first woman to discover a comet, eventually finding eight comets and three nebulae over her career. Working alongside her brother William, she became the first woman to receive a salary as a scientist when King George III appointed her as William's astronomical assistant. She was also the first woman to receive honorary membership in the Royal Astronomical Society and to receive a Gold Medal of the Royal Astronomical Society.
bioCn: 卡罗琳·赫歇尔是第一位发现彗星的女性，在她的职业生涯中共发现了八颗彗星和三个星云。与兄弟威廉一起工作，当乔治三世国王任命她为威廉的天文助手时，她成为第一位获得科学家薪水的女性。她也是第一位获得皇家天文学会荣誉会员和皇家天文学会金奖的女性。
achievements: First woman to discover a comet
Discovered 8 comets and 3 nebulae
First woman paid as a scientist by the British Crown
achievementsCn: 第一位发现彗星的女性
发现8颗彗星和3个星云
第一位获英国王室科学家薪水的女性
references: Hoskin, Michael. The Herschel Partnership. Science History Publications, 2003.

name: Henrietta Swan Leavitt
tagline: Discovered How to Measure the Universe
taglineCn: 发现了测量宇宙的方法
bio: Henrietta Swan Leavitt discovered the period-luminosity relationship of Cepheid variable stars — the brighter the star, the longer its pulsation period. This relationship became the first 'standard candle' for measuring cosmic distances, enabling Edwin Hubble to prove that the universe extends far beyond our galaxy. Hired as one of the 'Harvard Computers' — women who catalogued stars for minimal pay — her discovery fundamentally changed our understanding of the scale of the universe.
bioCn: 亨丽埃塔·勒维特发现了造父变星的周光关系——恒星越亮，其脉动周期越长。这一关系成为测量宇宙距离的第一个'标准烛光'，使哈勃得以证明宇宙远远超出我们的银河系。她被聘为'哈佛计算员'之一——为微薄报酬编目恒星的女性——她的发现从根本上改变了我们对宇宙尺度的理解。
achievements: Discovered the period-luminosity relationship of Cepheid stars
Enabled measurement of cosmic distances
Made possible the discovery of the expanding universe
achievementsCn: 发现造父变星的周光关系
使宇宙距离的测量成为可能
使发现宇宙膨胀成为可能
references: Johnson, George. Miss Leavitt's Stars. W.W. Norton, 2005.

name: Annie Jump Cannon
taglineCn: 分类了近40万颗恒星
bio: Annie Jump Cannon developed the Harvard spectral classification system (OBAFGKM) still used by astronomers today to classify stars by temperature. She manually classified nearly 400,000 stars — more than any other person in history — earning her the nickname 'Census Taker of the Sky.' Nearly deaf from childhood, she was one of the famous 'Harvard Computers' and became the first woman to receive an honorary doctorate from Oxford University.
bioCn: 安妮·坎农开发了哈佛光谱分类系统（OBAFGKM），至今仍被天文学家用来按温度分类恒星。她手动分类了近40万颗恒星——比历史上任何人都多——因此被称为'天空的人口普查员'。她从小近乎失聪，是著名的'哈佛计算员'之一，成为第一位获得牛津大学荣誉博士学位的女性。
achievements: Developed the Harvard spectral classification system
Classified nearly 400,000 stars
First woman to receive an honorary doctorate from Oxford
achievementsCn: 开发哈佛光谱分类系统
分类近40万颗恒星
第一位获牛津大学荣誉博士学位的女性
references: Sobel, Dava. The Glass Universe. Viking, 2016.

name: Williamina Fleming
taglineCn: 发现了马头星云
bio: Williamina Fleming was hired as a maid by astronomer Edward Pickering, who reportedly said his maid could do a better job than his male assistants. He was right. She became one of the first 'Harvard Computers' and developed a system for classifying stars by their spectra. She discovered the Horsehead Nebula, catalogued over 10,000 stars, and identified 59 gaseous nebulae, 310 variable stars, and 10 novae.
bioCn: 威廉明娜·弗莱明被天文学家爱德华·皮克林聘为女仆，据报道皮克林曾说他的女仆比他的男助手做得更好。他说对了。她成为最早的'哈佛计算员'之一，开发了一种通过光谱分类恒星的系统。她发现了马头星云，编目了超过10000颗恒星，鉴定了59个气体星云、310颗变星和10颗新星。
achievements: Discovered the Horsehead Nebula
Catalogued over 10,000 stars
Developed early stellar classification system
achievementsCn: 发现马头星云
编目超过10000颗恒星
开发早期恒星分类系统
references: Sobel, Dava. The Glass Universe. Viking, 2016.

name: Valentina Tereshkova
taglineCn: 第一位进入太空的女性
bio: Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman to fly in space on June 16, 1963, aboard Vostok 6. A former textile factory worker and amateur parachutist, she was selected from over 400 applicants for the Soviet cosmonaut program. She orbited Earth 48 times over nearly three days, logging more flight time than all American astronauts combined at that point. She remains the only woman to have completed a solo space mission.
bioCn: 瓦莲京娜·捷列什科娃于1963年6月16日乘坐东方6号飞船成为第一位飞入太空的女性。她曾是纺织厂工人和业余跳伞运动员，从400多名申请者中被选入苏联宇航员计划。她在近三天内绕地球飞行48圈，飞行时间超过了当时所有美国宇航员的总和。她至今仍是唯一一位完成独自太空任务的女性。
achievements: First woman in space (1963)
Orbited Earth 48 times in nearly 3 days
Only woman to have completed a solo space mission
achievementsCn: 第一位进入太空的女性（1963年）
在近3天内绕地球飞行48圈
唯一完成独自太空任务的女性
quote: Once you've been in space, you appreciate how small and fragile the Earth is.
quoteCn: 一旦你去过太空，就会体会到地球是多么渺小和脆弱。

name: Sally Ride
taglineCn: 第一位进入太空的美国女性
bio: Sally Ride became the first American woman in space when she flew on the Space Shuttle Challenger in 1983 at age 32. A physicist with a PhD from Stanford, she operated the shuttle's robotic arm to deploy satellites. After leaving NASA, she became a physics professor and founded Sally Ride Science, a company creating educational programs to inspire young people — especially girls — in STEM fields.
bioCn: 萨莉·赖德于1983年32岁时乘坐挑战者号航天飞机成为第一位进入太空的美国女性。她拥有斯坦福大学物理学博士学位，操作航天飞机的机械臂部署卫星。离开NASA后，她成为物理学教授并创办了Sally Ride Science，一家创建教育项目以激励年轻人——特别是女孩——投身STEM领域的公司。
achievements: First American woman in space (1983)
Youngest American astronaut at the time
Founded Sally Ride Science for STEM education
achievementsCn: 第一位进入太空的美国女性（1983年）
当时最年轻的美国宇航员
创办Sally Ride Science推广STEM教育
quote: All adventures, especially into new territory, are scary.
quoteCn: 所有的冒险，尤其是进入新领域的冒险，都是令人恐惧的。
references: Sherr, Lynn. Sally Ride: America's First Woman in Space. Simon & Schuster, 2014.

name: Mae Jemison
taglineCn: 第一位进入太空的非裔美国女性
bio: Mae Jemison became the first African-American woman to travel into space when she flew aboard Space Shuttle Endeavour in 1992. Before joining NASA, she earned degrees in chemical engineering and medicine, served in the Peace Corps as a medical officer in Sierra Leone, and worked as a general practitioner. After leaving NASA, she founded the 100 Year Starship project to make interstellar travel possible within a century.
bioCn: 梅·杰米森于1992年乘坐奋进号航天飞机成为第一位进入太空的非裔美国女性。在加入NASA之前，她获得了化学工程和医学学位，在塞拉利昂担任和平队医疗官，并从事全科医生工作。离开NASA后，她创立了100年星际飞船项目，旨在一个世纪内实现星际旅行。
achievements: First African-American woman in space (1992)
Engineer, physician, and astronaut
Founded 100 Year Starship project
achievementsCn: 第一位进入太空的非裔美国女性（1992年）
工程师、医生和宇航员
创立100年星际飞船项目
quote: Never limit yourself because of others' limited imagination; never limit others because of your own limited imagination.
quoteCn: 永远不要因为他人有限的想象力而限制自己；也永远不要因为自己有限的想象力而限制他人。
references: Jemison, Mae. Find Where the Wind Goes. Scholastic, 2001.

name: Liu Yang
taglineCn: 中国第一位女性宇航员
bio: Liu Yang became the first Chinese woman to travel to space in June 2012, aboard the Shenzhou 9 spacecraft. A former Air Force pilot with nearly 1,700 hours of flight experience, she was selected for China's astronaut program in 2010. She returned to space in 2022 aboard Shenzhou 14, serving as part of the crew that completed the assembly of the Tiangong space station.
bioCn: 刘洋于2012年6月乘坐神舟九号飞船成为中国第一位进入太空的女性。她曾是空军飞行员，拥有近1700小时的飞行经验，2010年被选入中国航天员计划。2022年她乘坐神舟十四号再次进入太空，作为完成天宫空间站组装的乘组成员。
achievements: First Chinese woman in space (2012)
Flew on both Shenzhou 9 and Shenzhou 14
Helped complete assembly of Tiangong space station
achievementsCn: 中国第一位进入太空的女性（2012年）
乘坐神舟九号和神舟十四号
帮助完成天宫空间站的组装

name: Peggy Whitson
taglineCn: 创纪录的宇航员
bio: Peggy Whitson holds the record for the most time spent in space by an American astronaut, accumulating 665 days across three missions. She was the first woman to command the International Space Station and the first female chief of the Astronaut Office at NASA. Growing up on a farm in Iowa, she watched the Apollo moon landings on television and decided to become an astronaut at age nine.
bioCn: 佩吉·惠特森保持着美国宇航员在太空停留时间最长的纪录，在三次任务中累计665天。她是第一位指挥国际空间站的女性，也是NASA宇航员办公室第一位女性负责人。她在爱荷华州的一个农场长大，在电视上观看了阿波罗登月任务，9岁时决定成为宇航员。
achievements: Most time in space by an American astronaut (665 days)
First woman to command the International Space Station
First female chief of NASA Astronaut Office
achievementsCn: 美国宇航员太空最长时间纪录（665天）
第一位指挥国际空间站的女性
NASA宇航员办公室第一位女性负责人

name: Carolyn Shoemaker
tagline: Discovered More Comets Than Any Living Person
taglineCn: 发现彗星数量超过任何在世者
bio: Carolyn Shoemaker discovered 32 comets and over 800 asteroids, more comets than any other living person at the time. She co-discovered Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9, which spectacularly crashed into Jupiter in 1994 — the first directly observed collision of two solar system objects. She began her career in astronomy at age 51, proving it is never too late to make world-changing discoveries.
bioCn: 卡罗琳·舒梅克发现了32颗彗星和800多颗小行星，当时发现的彗星数量超过任何在世者。她共同发现了舒梅克-列维9号彗星，1994年该彗星壮观地撞击了木星——这是第一次直接观测到的两个太阳系天体碰撞。她51岁才开始天文学职业生涯，证明了做出改变世界的发现永远不会太晚。
achievements: Discovered 32 comets — more than any living person at the time
Co-discovered Comet Shoemaker-Levy 9
Found over 800 asteroids
achievementsCn: 发现32颗彗星——当时超过任何在世者
共同发现舒梅克-列维9号彗星
发现800多颗小行星

name: Hedy Lamarr
tagline: Hollywood Star Who Invented Wireless Communication
taglineCn: 发明无线通信技术的好莱坞明星
bio: Hedy Lamarr was both a glamorous Hollywood actress and a brilliant inventor. During World War II, she co-invented a frequency-hopping spread spectrum communication system to prevent the jamming of torpedo guidance signals. Though the Navy initially dismissed her work, the principles she developed became the foundation for modern wireless technologies including Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and GPS. She was posthumously inducted into the National Inventors Hall of Fame.
bioCn: 海蒂·拉玛既是一位迷人的好莱坞女演员，也是一位杰出的发明家。二战期间，她共同发明了一种跳频扩频通信系统，用于防止鱼雷导引信号被干扰。虽然海军最初否定了她的工作，但她开发的原理成为了现代无线技术——包括Wi-Fi、蓝牙和GPS——的基础。她在去世后被追授入美国国家发明家名人堂。
achievements: Co-invented frequency-hopping spread spectrum technology
Foundation for Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and GPS
Inducted into National Inventors Hall of Fame
achievementsCn: 共同发明跳频扩频技术
Wi-Fi、蓝牙和GPS的基础
入选美国国家发明家名人堂
quote: All creative people want to do the unexpected.
quoteCn: 所有有创造力的人都想做出人意料的事。
references: Lamarr, H. & Antheil, G. US Patent 2,292,387: Secret Communication System, 1942.

name: Stephanie Kwolek
taglineCn: 凯夫拉的发明者
bio: Stephanie Kwolek invented Kevlar, a heat-resistant synthetic fiber five times stronger than steel by weight, while working at DuPont. Her discovery was accidental — she almost discarded the unusual solution she had created. Kevlar is now used in bulletproof vests, firefighter gear, suspension bridge cables, and spacecraft components. Her invention has saved countless lives and earned her the National Medal of Technology.
bioCn: 斯蒂芬妮·科沃莱克在杜邦工作时发明了凯夫拉——一种按重量计算是钢铁五倍强度的耐热合成纤维。她的发现是偶然的——她差点丢弃了她创造的异常溶液。凯夫拉现在被用于防弹衣、消防员装备、悬索桥缆绳和航天器部件。她的发明挽救了无数生命，为她赢得了国家技术奖章。
achievements: Invented Kevlar, five times stronger than steel
National Medal of Technology recipient
Inducted into National Inventors Hall of Fame
achievementsCn: 发明凯夫拉，强度是钢铁的五倍
获得国家技术奖章
入选国家发明家名人堂

name: Edith Clarke
taglineCn: 首位女性电气工程教授
bio: Edith Clarke was the first woman to earn a Master's degree in electrical engineering from MIT, the first female professionally employed electrical engineer in the United States, and the first female professor of electrical engineering in the country. She invented the Clarke calculator, a graphical device that solved equations involving electric current, and her textbook on power systems was widely used for decades.
bioCn: 伊迪丝·克拉克是第一位从MIT获得电气工程硕士学位的女性，美国第一位被专业雇用的女性电气工程师，也是美国第一位电气工程女性教授。她发明了克拉克计算器——一种解决电流方程的图形设备，她的电力系统教科书被广泛使用了几十年。
achievements: First woman with an EE Master's from MIT
First female electrical engineering professor in the U.S.
Invented the Clarke calculator
achievementsCn: 第一位从MIT获得电气工程硕士学位的女性
美国第一位女性电气工程教授
发明了克拉克计算器

name: Emily Warren Roebling
tagline: The Woman Who Built the Brooklyn Bridge
taglineCn: 建造布鲁克林大桥的女性
bio: Emily Warren Roebling took over the day-to-day supervision of the Brooklyn Bridge construction when her husband, the chief engineer, became bedridden with caisson disease. She studied higher mathematics, bridge engineering, and materials science to manage the project. For over a decade, she was the primary liaison between her husband and the engineers on site. She was the first person to cross the completed bridge in 1883.
bioCn: 当她的丈夫——首席工程师——因沉箱病卧床不起时，艾米丽·沃伦·罗布林接管了布鲁克林大桥建设的日常监督工作。她学习了高等数学、桥梁工程和材料科学来管理项目。十多年来，她是丈夫和现场工程师之间的主要联络人。1883年她是第一个走过建成的大桥的人。
achievements: Supervised construction of the Brooklyn Bridge
Self-taught bridge engineering and materials science
First person to cross the completed Brooklyn Bridge
achievementsCn: 监督布鲁克林大桥的建设
自学桥梁工程和材料科学
第一个走过建成的布鲁克林大桥的人
references: McCullough, David. The Great Bridge. Simon & Schuster, 1972.

name: Lillian Gilbreth
taglineCn: 现代管理之母
bio: Lillian Gilbreth was a pioneer of industrial engineering and management who applied psychology to workplace efficiency. She held a PhD in psychology, raised 12 children, and designed kitchens and household products that reduced physical strain — including the foot-pedal trash can and shelves inside refrigerator doors. She was the first woman elected to the National Academy of Engineering and the first woman to receive an honorary membership in the American Society of Mechanical Engineers.
bioCn: 莉莲·吉尔布雷思是工业工程和管理的先驱，将心理学应用于工作场所效率。她拥有心理学博士学位，养育了12个孩子，并设计了减轻体力负担的厨房和家用产品——包括脚踏式垃圾桶和冰箱门内搁架。她是第一位当选国家工程院院士的女性，也是第一位获得美国机械工程师协会荣誉会员的女性。
achievements: Pioneer of industrial engineering and ergonomics
First woman elected to National Academy of Engineering
Designed everyday products still used today
achievementsCn: 工业工程和人体工程学先驱
第一位当选国家工程院院士的女性
设计了至今仍在使用的日常产品
references: Lancaster, Jane. Making Time: Lillian Moller Gilbreth. Northeastern Univ Press, 2004.

name: Mary Anderson
taglineCn: 雨刮器的发明者
bio: Mary Anderson invented the first practical windshield wiper in 1903 after observing a streetcar driver struggling to see through sleet in New York City. She received a patent for her 'window cleaning device' — a spring-loaded arm with a rubber blade that could be operated from inside the vehicle. Though manufacturers initially showed no interest, her invention became standard equipment on all American cars by 1916.
bioCn: 玛丽·安德森于1903年观察到一位街车司机在纽约雨雪中挣扎看路后，发明了第一个实用的雨刮器。她为她的'车窗清洁装置'——一个带弹簧的臂和橡胶刮片，可从车内操作——获得了专利。虽然制造商最初没有兴趣，但到1916年她的发明成为所有美国汽车的标准设备。
achievements: Invented the windshield wiper (1903)
Patent for window cleaning device
Standard equipment on all American cars by 1916
achievementsCn: 发明雨刮器（1903年）
获得车窗清洁装置专利
1916年成为所有美国汽车标准设备

name: Beulah Louise Henry
taglineCn: 女爱迪生——49项专利和110项发明
bio: Beulah Louise Henry was dubbed 'Lady Edison' by the press for her prolific inventive output. She received 49 patents and is credited with approximately 110 inventions, spanning vacuum ice cream freezers, typewriter improvements, dolls with changeable eye colors, and a protograph that could produce four copies of a document without carbon paper. Despite having no formal engineering training, her mechanical intuition was extraordinary.
bioCn: 比尤拉·路易丝·亨利因其丰富的发明产出被媒体称为'女爱迪生'。她获得了49项专利，被认为有大约110项发明，涵盖真空冰淇淋冷冻机、打字机改进、可变换眼睛颜色的洋娃娃，以及无需碳纸即可复制四份文件的复印机。尽管没有正式的工程培训，她的机械直觉非常出众。
achievements: Received 49 patents for her inventions
Credited with approximately 110 inventions
Known as 'Lady Edison'
achievementsCn: 获得49项发明专利
被认为有大约110项发明
被称为'女爱迪生'

name: Ellen Swallow Richards
taglineCn: 环境工程先驱
bio: Ellen Swallow Richards was the first woman admitted to MIT, where she earned a degree in chemistry. She pioneered the field of sanitary engineering (now environmental engineering), conducting the first comprehensive water quality survey in the United States. Her work led to the first state water quality standards in America and the first modern sewage treatment plant. She also established the field of home economics as a science.
bioCn: 艾伦·斯沃洛·理查兹是第一位被MIT录取的女性，在那里获得了化学学位。她开创了卫生工程（现在的环境工程）领域，进行了美国第一次全面的水质调查。她的工作促成了美国第一个州水质标准和第一座现代污水处理厂。她还将家政学确立为一门科学。
achievements: First woman admitted to MIT
Pioneered environmental engineering in the U.S.
Conducted first comprehensive water quality survey
achievementsCn: 第一位被MIT录取的女性
在美国开创环境工程
进行了第一次全面水质调查
references: Clarke, Robert. Ellen Swallow: The Woman Who Founded Ecology. Follett, 1973.

name: Bessie Coleman
taglineCn: 第一位非裔美国女飞行员
bio: Bessie Coleman became the first African-American woman — and the first Native American — to hold a pilot's license. Denied admission to flight schools in the United States because of her race and gender, she learned French and traveled to France to earn her license in 1921. She became a celebrated barnstormer and refused to perform at venues that would not admit African Americans. She died in a plane crash at 34 while preparing for an aerial show.
bioCn: 贝西·科尔曼成为第一位获得飞行员执照的非裔美国女性——也是第一位美洲原住民。由于种族和性别原因被美国飞行学校拒绝后，她学习了法语，前往法国并于1921年获得执照。她成为著名的特技飞行员，拒绝在不接纳非裔美国人的场地表演。她34岁时在准备航空表演时因飞机失事去世。
achievements: First African-American and Native American woman pilot
Earned license in France after U.S. rejection
Pioneered racial integration in aviation
achievementsCn: 第一位非裔美国和美洲原住民女飞行员
在被美国拒绝后在法国获得执照
开创了航空界的种族融合
quote: I refused to take no for an answer.
quoteCn: 我拒绝接受否定的回答。
references: Rich, Doris L. Queen Bess: Daredevil Aviator. Smithsonian, 1993.

name: Beatrice Shilling
tagline: Engineer Who Saved RAF Pilots in WWII
taglineCn: 二战中拯救RAF飞行员的工程师
bio: Beatrice Shilling was a British aeronautical engineer whose simple yet brilliant invention saved countless RAF pilots' lives during World War II. She designed a small metal disc — nicknamed 'Miss Shilling's Orifice' — that prevented Merlin engines in Spitfires and Hurricanes from cutting out during negative-G maneuvers. She was also an accomplished motorcycle racer who earned the coveted Brooklands Gold Star.
bioCn: 比阿特丽斯·希林是一位英国航空工程师，她简单而巧妙的发明在二战中挽救了无数RAF飞行员的生命。她设计了一个小金属盘——被戏称为'希林小姐的孔'——防止喷火式和飓风式战斗机的梅林发动机在负G机动时熄火。她还是一位出色的摩托车赛车手，获得了令人梦寐以求的布鲁克兰兹金星。
achievements: Invented device saving RAF pilots in WWII
Solved engine failure problem in Spitfires and Hurricanes
Earned Brooklands Gold Star in motorcycle racing
achievementsCn: 发明了二战中挽救RAF飞行员的装置
解决了喷火式和飓风式战斗机的发动机故障问题
在摩托车赛中获得布鲁克兰兹金星

name: Gwynne Shotwell
taglineCn: SpaceX总裁
bio: Gwynne Shotwell has served as President and COO of SpaceX since 2008, managing day-to-day operations and business development for the company that has revolutionized space transportation. Under her leadership, SpaceX developed reusable rockets, launched astronauts to the International Space Station, and built the Starlink satellite network. She was inspired to pursue engineering after attending a talk by a woman mechanical engineer as a teenager.
bioCn: 格温·肖特韦尔自2008年以来一直担任SpaceX总裁兼首席运营官，管理这家彻底改变太空运输的公司的日常运营和业务发展。在她的领导下，SpaceX开发了可重复使用的火箭、将宇航员送入国际空间站、并建立了星链卫星网络。她是在少年时参加一位女机械工程师的演讲后受到启发而从事工程的。
achievements: President and COO of SpaceX since 2008
Led development of reusable rocket technology
Oversaw Starlink satellite network deployment
achievementsCn: 自2008年起担任SpaceX总裁兼COO
领导可重复使用火箭技术的开发
监督星链卫星网络部署

name: Limor Fried
taglineCn: 开源硬件先驱
bio: Limor Fried is the founder and lead engineer of Adafruit Industries, one of the world's leading open-source hardware companies. An MIT-trained engineer, she has been a leading voice in the maker movement and open-source hardware community. In 2012, she became the first female engineer on the cover of WIRED magazine. Adafruit has grown into a company that educates and empowers millions of people to learn electronics.
bioCn: 利莫·弗里德是Adafruit Industries的创始人和首席工程师，这是世界领先的开源硬件公司之一。作为MIT培养的工程师，她一直是创客运动和开源硬件社区的领军人物。2012年，她成为第一位登上《连线》杂志封面的女性工程师。Adafruit已发展成为一家教育和赋能数百万人学习电子技术的公司。
achievements: Founded Adafruit Industries
First female engineer on the cover of WIRED
Pioneer of open-source hardware movement
achievementsCn: 创立Adafruit Industries
第一位登上《连线》杂志封面的女性工程师
开源硬件运动先驱

name: Eunice Newton Foote
tagline: First to Discover the Greenhouse Effect
taglineCn: 第一个发现温室效应的人
bio: Eunice Newton Foote was the first scientist to demonstrate the greenhouse effect of carbon dioxide. In 1856 — three years before John Tyndall's more famous experiments — she showed that a cylinder of CO2 heated more in sunlight than one filled with air, and that it cooled more slowly. As a woman, she was not allowed to present her own paper at the AAAS meeting; a male colleague read it for her. Her work was forgotten for over 150 years.
bioCn: 尤尼斯·牛顿·富特是第一位证明二氧化碳温室效应的科学家。1856年——比约翰·廷德尔更著名的实验早三年——她表明装有CO2的圆筒在阳光下比装满空气的加热更多，冷却更慢。作为一名女性，她不被允许在AAAS会议上宣读自己的论文；由一位男同事代为宣读。她的工作被遗忘了150多年。
achievements: First to demonstrate CO2 greenhouse effect (1856)
Preceded Tyndall's experiments by three years
Work rediscovered after 150+ years
achievementsCn: 第一个证明CO2温室效应（1856年）
比廷德尔的实验早三年
工作在150多年后被重新发现
references: Foote, E.N. 'Circumstances Affecting the Heat of the Sun's Rays,' American Journal of Science, 1856.

name: Inge Lehmann
taglineCn: 发现了地球内核
bio: Inge Lehmann was a Danish seismologist who discovered that Earth has a solid inner core within its liquid outer core. By analyzing seismic waves from earthquakes, she proposed in 1936 that the Earth's core is not a single molten sphere, as previously believed, but has a solid inner part. She lived to 104, making her one of the longest-lived scientists in history, and continued publishing research into her 70s.
bioCn: 英格·莱曼是一位丹麦地震学家，她发现地球在液态外核内有一个固态内核。通过分析地震产生的地震波，她于1936年提出地球的核心不像之前认为的那样是单一的熔融球体，而是有一个固态的内部。她活到了104岁，成为历史上最长寿的科学家之一，70多岁仍在发表研究。
achievements: Discovered Earth's solid inner core (1936)
Lived to age 104
Fundamentally changed understanding of Earth's structure
achievementsCn: 发现地球固态内核（1936年）
活到104岁
从根本上改变了对地球结构的理解
references: Lehmann, I. 'P',' Publications du Bureau Central Séismologique International, 1936.

name: Marie Tharp
taglineCn: 绘制了海底地图
bio: Marie Tharp created the first comprehensive map of the ocean floor, revealing the Mid-Atlantic Ridge and providing crucial evidence for the theory of plate tectonics and continental drift. Banned from research ships because she was a woman, she worked from shore analyzing sonar data. When she first identified the rift valley, her colleague Bruce Heezen dismissed her findings as 'girl talk.' Her maps fundamentally changed our understanding of Earth's geology.
bioCn: 玛丽·萨普创建了第一张综合性海底地图，揭示了大西洋中脊，为板块构造和大陆漂移理论提供了关键证据。因为她是女性而被禁止上研究船，她在岸上工作分析声纳数据。当她首次发现裂谷时，她的同事布鲁斯·黑曾将她的发现斥为'女人话'。她的地图从根本上改变了我们对地球地质的理解。
achievements: Created first comprehensive map of the ocean floor
Discovered the Mid-Atlantic Ridge rift valley
Provided key evidence for plate tectonics theory
achievementsCn: 创建第一张综合性海底地图
发现大西洋中脊裂谷
为板块构造理论提供关键证据
references: Felt, Hali. Soundings: The Story of the Remarkable Woman Who Mapped the Ocean Floor. Henry Holt, 2012.

name: Wangari Maathai
