tagline: Nobel Peace Prize for Environmental Conservation
taglineCn: 因环境保护获诺贝尔和平奖
bio: Wangari Maathai was the first African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize (2004) for her contribution to sustainable development, democracy, and peace through her Green Belt Movement. She mobilized rural women across Kenya to plant over 51 million trees, combating deforestation and providing livelihoods. She was also the first woman in East and Central Africa to earn a PhD and the first female professor in Kenya.
bioCn: 旺加里·马塔伊是第一位获得诺贝尔和平奖（2004年）的非洲女性，表彰她通过绿带运动对可持续发展、民主和和平的贡献。她动员肯尼亚各地的农村妇女种植了超过5100万棵树，对抗森林砍伐并提供生计。她还是东非和中非第一位获得博士学位的女性，也是肯尼亚第一位女性教授。
achievements: Nobel Peace Prize (2004) for environmental conservation
Green Belt Movement planted 51+ million trees
First African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize
achievementsCn: 因环境保护获诺贝尔和平奖（2004年）
绿带运动种植超过5100万棵树
第一位获诺贝尔和平奖的非洲女性
quote: It's the little things citizens do. That's what will make the difference. My little thing is planting trees.
quoteCn: 正是公民做的小事会带来改变。我的小事就是种树。
references: Maathai, W. Unbowed: A Memoir. Knopf, 2006.

name: Sylvia Earle
tagline: Her Deepness — Queen of the Deep
taglineCn: 深海女王
bio: Sylvia Earle is a marine biologist, explorer, and author who has been at the forefront of ocean exploration for over five decades. In 1979, she walked untethered on the sea floor at 381 meters depth — deeper than any human before. Known as 'Her Deepness,' she was the first female chief scientist of NOAA and has led over 100 expeditions. She founded Mission Blue, a global initiative to establish marine protected areas.
bioCn: 西尔维亚·厄尔是一位海洋生物学家、探险家和作家，五十多年来一直处于海洋探索的最前沿。1979年，她在381米深处无系绳行走于海底——比之前任何人都深。被称为'深海女王'，她是NOAA第一位女性首席科学家，领导了100多次探险。她创立了Mission Blue，一个建立海洋保护区的全球倡议。
achievements: Set solo ocean depth record at 381 meters
First female chief scientist of NOAA
Led over 100 ocean expeditions
achievementsCn: 创下381米独自海底深潜纪录
NOAA第一位女性首席科学家
领导100多次海洋探险
quote: No water, no life. No blue, no green.
quoteCn: 没有水就没有生命。没有蓝色就没有绿色。
references: Earle, Sylvia. The World Is Blue. National Geographic, 2009.

name: Katia Krafft
taglineCn: 火山学先驱
bio: Katia Krafft was a French volcanologist who, together with her husband Maurice, documented volcanic eruptions around the world with extraordinary courage and scientific rigor. Their footage and photographs of eruptions from dangerously close range have been invaluable for volcanology and public safety. Their film of pyroclastic flows directly influenced evacuation procedures that saved tens of thousands of lives during the 1991 Mount Pinatubo eruption. Tragically, both Kraffts died in a pyroclastic flow at Mount Unzen, Japan, in 1991.
bioCn: 卡提亚·克拉夫特是一位法国火山学家，与丈夫莫里斯一起以非凡的勇气和科学严谨性记录了世界各地的火山喷发。他们在危险近距离拍摄的火山喷发影像和照片对火山学和公共安全具有无价的价值。他们拍摄的火山碎屑流影片直接影响了1991年皮纳图博火山喷发时的疏散程序，拯救了数万人的生命。不幸的是，两位克拉夫特于1991年在日本云仙岳的一次火山碎屑流中遇难。
achievements: Pioneered close-range volcanic documentation
Footage saved tens of thousands during Pinatubo eruption
Documented over 150 volcanic eruptions worldwide
achievementsCn: 开创近距离火山记录
影像在皮纳图博喷发期间拯救了数万人
在全球记录了150多次火山喷发

name: Mary-Claire King
taglineCn: 发现了BRCA1癌症基因
bio: Mary-Claire King proved that breast cancer can be inherited through a single gene — BRCA1 — revolutionizing cancer research and prevention. Her discovery, made in 1990 after 17 years of research, enabled genetic testing that has saved countless lives through early detection. She also proved that humans and chimpanzees are 99% genetically identical and used DNA analysis to identify victims of Argentina's 'Dirty War,' reuniting stolen children with their biological families.
bioCn: 玛丽-克莱尔·金证明了乳腺癌可以通过单一基因——BRCA1——遗传，革新了癌症研究和预防。她的发现是在17年研究之后于1990年做出的，使基因检测成为可能，通过早期检测挽救了无数生命。她还证明人类和黑猩猩在基因上有99%相同，并利用DNA分析鉴定阿根廷'肮脏战争'的受害者，帮助被盗儿童与亲生家庭团聚。
achievements: Discovered BRCA1 breast cancer gene
Proved 99% genetic similarity between humans and chimps
Used DNA to identify victims of Argentina's Dirty War
achievementsCn: 发现BRCA1乳腺癌基因
证明人类和黑猩猩99%基因相似
利用DNA鉴定阿根廷肮脏战争受害者
references: King, M.C. et al. 'Breast and Ovarian Cancer Risks Due to Inherited Mutations in BRCA1 and BRCA2,' Science, 2003.

name: Katalin Karikó
taglineCn: mRNA技术先驱
bio: Katalin Karikó spent decades researching mRNA technology despite repeated rejections, demotions, and funding denials. Her persistence paid off spectacularly when her foundational work on modified mRNA enabled the rapid development of the Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna COVID-19 vaccines, which have been administered billions of times worldwide. She received the 2023 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, vindicating decades of underfunded, underappreciated research.
bioCn: 卡塔林·卡里科尽管一再遭到拒绝、降职和资金否决，仍花了数十年研究mRNA技术。她的坚持获得了惊人的回报——她在修饰mRNA方面的基础工作使辉瑞-BioNTech和Moderna新冠疫苗得以快速开发，这些疫苗已在全球接种数十亿次。她获得了2023年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖，证明了数十年来资金不足、不受重视的研究的价值。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2023)
Foundational mRNA research enabled COVID-19 vaccines
Vaccines administered billions of times worldwide
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2023年）
基础mRNA研究使新冠疫苗成为可能
疫苗在全球接种数十亿次
quote: I thought of leaving science many times, but I always went back to the bench.
quoteCn: 我多次想过离开科学，但总是回到了实验台前。

name: Flossie Wong-Staal
tagline: First to Clone and Map HIV Genome
taglineCn: 首次克隆和绘制HIV基因组图谱
bio: Flossie Wong-Staal was the first scientist to clone HIV and determine the function of its genes, work that was essential for proving HIV causes AIDS and for developing the first HIV blood test. Born in Guangzhou, China, she moved to the United States and became one of the most cited scientists in the world. She later developed a novel approach to gene therapy and co-founded a biotech company focused on treating hepatitis C.
bioCn: 黄以静是第一位克隆HIV并确定其基因功能的科学家，这一工作对证明HIV导致AIDS以及开发第一个HIV血液检测至关重要。她出生于中国广州，移居美国后成为世界上被引用最多的科学家之一。她后来开发了一种新的基因治疗方法，并共同创办了一家专注于治疗丙型肝炎的生物技术公司。
achievements: First to clone HIV and map its genome
Work essential for first HIV blood test
Among most cited scientists in the world
achievementsCn: 首次克隆HIV并绘制其基因组图谱
工作对第一个HIV血液检测至关重要
世界上被引用最多的科学家之一

name: Patricia Bath
taglineCn: 发明了激光白内障手术
bio: Patricia Bath was an ophthalmologist and inventor who created the Laserphaco Probe, a device that uses laser technology to remove cataracts and restore sight. She was the first African-American woman to complete a residency in ophthalmology, the first to receive a medical patent, and the first African-American woman doctor to receive a patent for a medical purpose. Her invention has restored sight to people who had been blind for over 30 years.
bioCn: 帕特里夏·巴斯是一位眼科医生和发明家，她创造了Laserphaco探针——一种使用激光技术去除白内障并恢复视力的设备。她是第一位完成眼科住院医师培训的非裔美国女性，第一位获得医学专利的非裔美国女性，也是第一位因医疗目的获得专利的非裔美国女医生。她的发明已为失明超过30年的人恢复了视力。
achievements: Invented Laserphaco Probe for cataract surgery
First African-American woman with a medical patent
Restored sight to people blind for 30+ years
achievementsCn: 发明了用于白内障手术的Laserphaco探针
第一位获得医学专利的非裔美国女性
为失明超过30年的人恢复了视力
references: Bath, P. US Patent 4,744,360: Apparatus for ablating and removing cataract lenses, 1988.

name: Esther Lederberg
taglineCn: 细菌遗传学先驱
bio: Esther Lederberg made foundational discoveries in bacterial genetics, including the discovery of lambda phage (a virus that infects bacteria) and the invention of replica plating, a technique still used in microbiology labs worldwide. Despite these breakthrough contributions, the 1958 Nobel Prize for work on bacterial genetics was awarded only to her husband Joshua Lederberg. Her techniques remain essential tools in genetics and molecular biology research.
bioCn: 埃丝特·莱德伯格在细菌遗传学方面做出了奠基性发现，包括发现λ噬菌体（一种感染细菌的病毒）和发明复制平板法——一种至今仍在全世界微生物学实验室使用的技术。尽管她做出了突破性贡献，1958年细菌遗传学方面的诺贝尔奖仅授予了她的丈夫约书亚·莱德伯格。她的技术至今仍是遗传学和分子生物学研究的基本工具。
achievements: Discovered lambda phage
Invented replica plating technique
Foundational contributions to bacterial genetics
achievementsCn: 发现λ噬菌体
发明复制平板法
对细菌遗传学做出奠基性贡献

name: Dorothy Vaughan
taglineCn: NASA第一位非裔美国管理者
bio: Dorothy Vaughan was a mathematician and NASA's first African-American manager. She led the West Area Computers, a group of African-American women mathematicians who performed complex calculations for the space program during the era of segregation. When electronic computers arrived, she taught herself and her team FORTRAN programming, ensuring they remained vital to NASA's operations. Her story was told in the book and film Hidden Figures.
bioCn: 多萝西·沃恩是一位数学家和NASA第一位非裔美国管理者。在种族隔离时代，她领导西区计算员——一群为太空项目进行复杂计算的非裔美国女数学家。当电子计算机到来时，她自学并教团队FORTRAN编程，确保她们对NASA的运营仍然至关重要。她的故事在书籍和电影《隐藏人物》中被讲述。
achievements: NASA's first African-American manager
Led West Area Computers group
Self-taught FORTRAN programming to adapt to electronic computers
achievementsCn: NASA第一位非裔美国管理者
领导西区计算员团队
自学FORTRAN编程以适应电子计算机
references: Shetterly, Margot Lee. Hidden Figures. William Morrow, 2016.

name: Annie Easley
tagline: NASA Computer Scientist and Rocket Pioneer
taglineCn: NASA计算机科学家和火箭先驱
bio: Annie Easley was an African-American computer scientist and mathematician who worked at NASA for 34 years. She developed and implemented code for the Centaur rocket stage, which launched the first missions to explore other planets. She also contributed to energy-conversion systems and battery technology that laid the groundwork for hybrid vehicles. Throughout her career, she faced racial discrimination but remained a fierce advocate for equal opportunity in STEM.
bioCn: 安妮·伊斯利是一位非裔美国计算机科学家和数学家，在NASA工作了34年。她为半人马座火箭级开发和实施了代码，该火箭发射了探索其他行星的第一批任务。她还为能量转换系统和电池技术做出了贡献，这为混合动力汽车奠定了基础。在她的整个职业生涯中，她面临种族歧视但始终是STEM领域机会平等的坚定倡导者。
achievements: Developed code for the Centaur rocket stage
Contributed to battery technology for hybrid vehicles
34-year career at NASA as a computer scientist
achievementsCn: 为半人马座火箭级开发代码
为混合动力汽车的电池技术做出贡献
在NASA担任34年计算机科学家
references: NASA Glenn Research Center oral history, 2001

name: Mary Allen Wilkes
taglineCn: 第一位家用电脑用户
bio: Mary Allen Wilkes was a pioneering computer programmer who became the first person to have a personal computer in her home. While working at MIT Lincoln Laboratory, she designed the operating system for the LINC, often considered the first minicomputer. In 1965, she took a LINC home to continue her work — years before personal computers existed commercially. She later became a lawyer, but her early work helped envision the personal computing revolution.
bioCn: 玛丽·艾伦·威尔克斯是一位开创性的计算机程序员，她成为第一个在家中拥有个人电脑的人。在MIT林肯实验室工作时，她为LINC设计了操作系统——LINC通常被认为是第一台小型计算机。1965年，她把一台LINC带回家继续工作——比个人电脑商业化早了好几年。她后来成为律师，但她的早期工作帮助构想了个人计算革命。
achievements: First person to use a computer at home (1965)
Designed the operating system for the LINC minicomputer
Pioneered the concept of personal computing
achievementsCn: 第一个在家使用电脑的人（1965年）
为LINC小型计算机设计操作系统
开创了个人计算的概念

name: Evelyn Boyd Granville
nameCn: 伊芙琳·博伊德·格兰维尔
tagline: Second African-American Woman to Earn a Math PhD
taglineCn: 第二位获得数学博士学位的非裔美国女性
bio: Evelyn Boyd Granville was the second African-American woman to receive a PhD in mathematics in the United States, earning her degree from Yale in 1949. She worked at IBM and later at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, where she developed computer programs for trajectory analysis during the Mercury and Apollo missions. She spent the latter part of her career teaching mathematics at universities, inspiring future generations.
bioCn: 伊芙琳·博伊德·格兰维尔是美国第二位获得数学博士学位的非裔美国女性，1949年从耶鲁大学获得学位。她在IBM和后来的NASA喷气推进实验室工作，在那里她为水星和阿波罗任务开发了轨迹分析计算机程序。她职业生涯的后期在大学教授数学，激励了未来的世代。
achievements: Second African-American woman with a math PhD from Yale (1949)
Developed trajectory programs for Mercury and Apollo missions
Taught mathematics at multiple universities
achievementsCn: 第二位从耶鲁获得数学博士的非裔美国女性（1949年）
为水星和阿波罗任务开发轨迹程序
在多所大学教授数学

name: Gladys West
taglineCn: GPS背后的数学头脑
bio: Gladys West was a mathematician whose work at the Naval Surface Warfare Center was critical to the development of the Global Positioning System (GPS). She programmed an IBM computer to deliver precise calculations for an accurate geodetic Earth model, which became the basis for GPS technology. Her contributions were unknown to the public for decades until she was inducted into the Air Force Space and Missile Pioneers Hall of Fame in 2018.
bioCn: 格拉迪丝·韦斯特是一位数学家，她在海军水面作战中心的工作对全球定位系统（GPS）的开发至关重要。她为IBM计算机编程，提供精确计算以建立准确的大地测量地球模型，这成为了GPS技术的基础。她的贡献在几十年里不为公众所知，直到2018年她入选空军太空和导弹先驱名人堂。
achievements: Mathematical modeling critical to GPS development
Inducted into Air Force Space and Missile Pioneers Hall of Fame
Programmed precise geodetic Earth model
achievementsCn: 对GPS开发至关重要的数学建模
入选空军太空和导弹先驱名人堂
编程精确的大地测量地球模型

name: Cynthia Breazeal
taglineCn: 社交机器人先驱
bio: Cynthia Breazeal is a roboticist and professor at MIT who pioneered the field of social robotics. She designed Kismet, one of the first robots capable of recognizing and simulating human emotions, and later created Jibo, described as the world's first social robot for the home. Her work explores how robots can interact with humans as collaborative partners rather than mere tools.
bioCn: 辛西娅·布雷齐尔是MIT的机器人学家和教授，开创了社交机器人领域。她设计了Kismet——第一批能够识别和模拟人类情感的机器人之一，后来又创造了Jibo——被描述为世界上第一个家用社交机器人。她的工作探索机器人如何作为协作伙伴而非仅仅是工具与人类互动。
achievements: Created Kismet, early robot recognizing human emotions
Developed Jibo, first social robot for the home
Pioneered human-robot interaction research at MIT
achievementsCn: 创造Kismet，早期识别人类情感的机器人
开发Jibo，第一个家用社交机器人
在MIT开创人机交互研究
references: Breazeal, C. Designing Sociable Robots. MIT Press, 2002.

name: Daphne Koller
taglineCn: AI先驱与Coursera联合创始人
bio: Daphne Koller is a computer scientist whose work on probabilistic graphical models and machine learning has shaped modern artificial intelligence. As a Stanford professor, she co-founded Coursera in 2012, democratizing education by making university-level courses available to millions worldwide for free. She was awarded the MacArthur Fellowship and is a member of the National Academy of Sciences. She later founded Insitro, applying machine learning to drug discovery.
bioCn: 达芙妮·科勒是一位计算机科学家，她在概率图模型和机器学习方面的工作塑造了现代人工智能。作为斯坦福教授，她于2012年联合创立Coursera，通过向全球数百万人免费提供大学级课程来实现教育民主化。她获得了麦克阿瑟奖并是国家科学院成员。她后来创立了Insitro，将机器学习应用于药物发现。
achievements: Co-founded Coursera, educating millions worldwide
MacArthur Fellowship recipient
Pioneer of probabilistic graphical models in AI
achievementsCn: 联合创立Coursera，教育全球数百万人
获得麦克阿瑟奖
AI概率图模型先驱

name: Joy Buolamwini
nationalityCn: 加纳裔加拿大裔美国人
taglineCn: 算法公正倡导者
bio: Joy Buolamwini is a computer scientist and digital activist who founded the Algorithmic Justice League after discovering significant racial and gender bias in commercial facial recognition systems. Her MIT research showed that facial analysis software from major tech companies had error rates up to 34% higher for darker-skinned women compared to lighter-skinned men. Her work has led to concrete policy changes and corporate accountability in AI development.
bioCn: 乔伊·布兰维尼是一位计算机科学家和数字活动家，在发现商业面部识别系统中存在严重的种族和性别偏见后，创立了算法正义联盟。她在MIT的研究表明，主要科技公司的面部分析软件对深肤色女性的错误率比浅肤色男性高出34%。她的工作促成了AI开发方面的具体政策变化和企业问责。
achievements: Founded the Algorithmic Justice League
Exposed racial and gender bias in AI facial recognition
Influenced policy changes in AI development
achievementsCn: 创立算法正义联盟
揭露AI面部识别中的种族和性别偏见
影响AI开发的政策变化
references: Buolamwini, J. & Gebru, T. 'Gender Shades,' Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, 2018.

name: Timnit Gebru
taglineCn: AI伦理领袖
bio: Timnit Gebru is a computer scientist and advocate for ethical AI who co-founded Black in AI, a community of Black researchers in artificial intelligence. Her research highlighted the social and environmental costs of large language models and the disproportionate harm AI systems can cause to marginalized communities. She founded the Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR) to conduct independent, community-rooted AI research.
bioCn: 蒂姆尼特·格布鲁是一位计算机科学家和AI伦理倡导者，她联合创立了Black in AI——一个人工智能领域黑人研究者社区。她的研究突出了大型语言模型的社会和环境成本，以及AI系统可能对边缘化社区造成的不成比例的伤害。她创立了分布式AI研究所（DAIR），进行独立的、以社区为根基的AI研究。
achievements: Co-founded Black in AI community
Founded Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR)
Highlighted social costs of large language models
achievementsCn: 联合创立Black in AI社区
创立分布式AI研究所（DAIR）
突出大型语言模型的社会成本

name: Lisa Randall
taglineCn: 探索额外维度的理论物理学家
bio: Lisa Randall is a theoretical physicist at Harvard University whose work on particle physics and cosmology has proposed models involving extra dimensions of space. Her Randall-Sundrum model, developed with Raman Sundrum, suggests that our visible universe exists on a membrane within a higher-dimensional space. She is one of the most cited theoretical physicists in the world and has authored popular science books making complex physics accessible to general audiences.
bioCn: 丽莎·兰道尔是哈佛大学的理论物理学家，她在粒子物理学和宇宙学方面的工作提出了涉及空间额外维度的模型。她与拉曼·桑德鲁姆共同开发的兰道尔-桑德鲁姆模型表明，我们可见的宇宙存在于更高维空间中的一个膜上。她是世界上被引用最多的理论物理学家之一，还撰写了科普书籍，使复杂的物理学对普通读者变得易于理解。
achievements: Developed the Randall-Sundrum model of extra dimensions
Most cited theoretical physicist in the world
Best-selling author making physics accessible
achievementsCn: 开发兰道尔-桑德鲁姆额外维度模型
世界上被引用最多的理论物理学家
畅销书作者，使物理学变得易懂
references: Randall, L. Warped Passages. Ecco Press, 2005.

name: Sophie Wilson
tagline: Architect of the ARM Instruction Set
taglineCn: ARM指令集架构师
bio: Sophie Wilson designed the instruction set for the ARM processor while working at Acorn Computers in the 1980s. The ARM architecture she created went on to become the most widely used processor design in the world, powering billions of smartphones, tablets, and embedded devices. She also designed the BBC Micro computer, which introduced a generation of British children to computing. Wilson was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society in 2019 and appointed CBE.
bioCn: 苏菲·威尔逊在1980年代于Acorn计算机公司工作期间设计了ARM处理器的指令集。她创造的ARM架构后来成为世界上使用最广泛的处理器设计，驱动着数十亿部智能手机、平板电脑和嵌入式设备。她还设计了BBC Micro计算机，将一代英国儿童引入了计算机世界。威尔逊于2019年当选皇家学会院士，并被授予CBE勋章。
achievements: Designed the ARM instruction set, used in billions of devices
Created the BBC Micro computer
Elected Fellow of the Royal Society (2019)
achievementsCn: 设计了ARM指令集，用于数十亿设备
创造了BBC Micro计算机
当选皇家学会院士（2019年）
references: Acorn Computers archive, Centre for Computing History

name: Dame Stephanie Shirley
tagline: Software Industry Pioneer Who Signed Letters as 'Steve'
taglineCn: 以'Steve'署名的软件产业先驱
bio: Born Vera Buchthal in Dortmund, Germany, Stephanie Shirley arrived in Britain as a Kindertransport child refugee in 1939. In 1962, she founded Freelance Programmers — a software company staffed almost entirely by women working from home, decades before remote work became mainstream. She adopted the name 'Steve' to combat the sexism that prevented her business letters from receiving replies. Her company, later renamed FI Group, grew to employ over 8,500 people and was valued at $3 billion. She has since donated over £67 million to charity, particularly for autism research.
bioCn: 斯蒂芬妮·雪莉原名维拉·布赫塔尔，出生于德国多特蒙德，1939年作为儿童运输计划的难民抵达英国。1962年，她创办了自由程序员公司——一家几乎全由女性在家办公的软件公司，比远程办公成为主流早了几十年。她采用'Steve'这个名字来对抗性别歧视，因为她以女性名字寄出的商业信函总是得不到回复。她的公司后来更名为FI Group，员工超过8,500人，市值达30亿美元。此后她已捐赠超过6,700万英镑用于慈善事业，特别是自闭症研究。
achievements: Founded Freelance Programmers (1962), pioneering remote work
Built FI Group into a $3 billion company
Donated over £67 million to charity
achievementsCn: 创办自由程序员公司（1962年），开创远程工作模式
将FI Group发展为价值30亿美元的公司
捐赠超过6,700万英镑用于慈善
quote: In those days, I had to use the name Steve to get past the door.
quoteCn: 在那个年代，我不得不用Steve这个名字才能敲开商业的大门。
references: Shirley, Stephanie. Let It Go: My Extraordinary Story. Penguin, 2012.
TED Talk: Why do ambitious women have flat heads? 2015

name: Anita Borg
tagline: Founded the Grace Hopper Celebration of Women in Computing
taglineCn: 创办女性计算机大会Grace Hopper Celebration
bio: Anita Borg was a computer scientist whose passion for increasing the representation of women in technology led her to create lasting institutions. In 1987, she founded Systers, the first electronic mailing list for women in computing, which grew to become a global network. In 1994, she co-founded the Grace Hopper Celebration of Women in Computing, now the world's largest gathering of women technologists. In 1997, she established the Institute for Women and Technology (now AnitaB.org). She received the Augusta Ada Lovelace Award and the EFF Pioneer Award before her death from brain cancer at 54.
bioCn: 安妮塔·博格是一位计算机科学家，她对增加女性在技术领域代表性的热情促使她创建了持久的机构。1987年，她创办了Systers——第一个面向计算机领域女性的电子邮件列表，发展成为一个全球网络。1994年，她联合创办了Grace Hopper女性计算机大会，现已成为全球最大的女性技术人员聚会。1997年，她创立了女性与技术研究所（现AnitaB.org）。她在54岁因脑癌去世前，获得了阿达·洛芙莱斯奖和EFF先驱奖。
achievements: Created Systers mailing list for women in computing (1987)
Co-founded the Grace Hopper Celebration (1994)
Founded the Institute for Women and Technology
achievementsCn: 创建了女性计算机领域的Systers邮件列表（1987年）
联合创办Grace Hopper Celebration（1994年）
创立女性与技术研究所

name: Joan Clarke
tagline: Bletchley Park Codebreaker Who Cracked Enigma
taglineCn: 布莱切利园密码破译者
bio: Joan Clarke was a cryptanalyst at Bletchley Park during World War II, where she worked in Hut 8 on breaking German Naval Enigma codes alongside Alan Turing. She was the only woman in Hut 8 to be promoted to the senior role of linguist — a deliberately misleading title, as the civil service had no protocols for women in such positions. She used the Banburismus statistical technique to crack Enigma settings. After the war, she worked at GCHQ. Her story gained wider recognition through the 2014 film The Imitation Game, where she was portrayed by Keira Knightley.
bioCn: 琼·克拉克是二战期间布莱切利园的密码分析员，她在第8小屋与艾伦·图灵一起破解德国海军恩尼格玛密码。她是第8小屋中唯一被提升为高级'语言学家'的女性——这是一个故意误导的头衔，因为公务员系统没有女性担任此类职位的先例。她使用班布里斯姆斯统计技术破解恩尼格玛设置。战后，她在政府通信总部(GCHQ)工作。她的故事通过2014年电影《模仿游戏》获得了更广泛的认知。
achievements: Broke German Naval Enigma codes at Bletchley Park
Only woman promoted to senior role in Hut 8
Worked alongside Alan Turing on cryptanalysis
achievementsCn: 在布莱切利园破解德国海军恩尼格玛密码
第8小屋中唯一晋升高级职位的女性
与艾伦·图灵一起从事密码分析工作
references: Smith, Michael. The Debs of Bletchley Park. Aurum Press, 2015.

name: Susan Kare
tagline: The Woman Who Gave the Macintosh a Smile
taglineCn: 赋予Macintosh微笑的女人
bio: Susan Kare is the graphic designer who created the original icons, fonts, and interface elements for the Apple Macintosh in 1983. Working on a pixel grid, she crafted the Happy Mac startup icon, the command key symbol, the bomb error icon, and bitmap fonts like Chicago and Geneva that became integral to the Mac identity. Her work established the visual language of personal computing and influenced user interface design worldwide. She went on to design icons for Windows 3.0, IBM, and Facebook, and was inducted into the AIGA Medal hall of fame.
bioCn: 苏珊·卡尔是图形设计师，1983年为Apple Macintosh创造了原始图标、字体和界面元素。她在像素网格上精心设计了Happy Mac启动图标、command键符号、炸弹错误图标，以及Chicago和Geneva等位图字体，这些都成为Mac身份的核心组成部分。她的工作确立了个人计算的视觉语言，影响了全球的用户界面设计。她后来为Windows 3.0、IBM和Facebook设计了图标，并入选AIGA奖章名人堂。
achievements: Created the original Macintosh icons and fonts
Designed the Happy Mac, command key symbol, and bomb error icon
Established the visual language of personal computing
achievementsCn: 创造了原始Macintosh图标和字体
设计了Happy Mac、command键符号和炸弹错误图标
确立了个人计算的视觉语言
references: Hertzfeld, Andy. Revolution in the Valley. O'Reilly, 2004.

name: Carol Shaw
tagline: One of the First Female Video Game Designers
taglineCn: 最早的女性电子游戏设计师之一
bio: Carol Shaw is recognized as one of the first female video game designers in the industry. After studying computer science and electrical engineering at UC Berkeley, she joined Atari in 1978, where she programmed games for the Atari 2600 including 3-D Tic-Tac-Toe. She later moved to Activision, where she created River Raid (1982), a critically acclaimed and commercially successful scrolling shooter that became one of the best-selling games of its era. River Raid was notable for its procedural level generation algorithm, a sophisticated technique for its time.
bioCn: 卡罗尔·肖被认为是电子游戏行业最早的女性游戏设计师之一。在加州大学伯克利分校学习计算机科学和电气工程后，她于1978年加入Atari，为Atari 2600编程游戏。她后来转到Activision，创作了《River Raid》（1982年），这款广受好评且商业成功的卷轴射击游戏成为那个时代最畅销的游戏之一。《River Raid》以其程序化关卡生成算法而著称，这在当时是一种先进的技术。
achievements: Created River Raid, a landmark video game (1982)
One of the first women in the video game industry
Pioneered procedural level generation
achievementsCn: 创作了里程碑式电子游戏《River Raid》（1982年）
电子游戏行业最早的女性之一
开创了程序化关卡生成技术
references: Donovan, Tristan. Replay: The History of Video Games. Yellow Ant, 2010.

name: Evelyn Berezin
tagline: Creator of the First Computerized Word Processor
taglineCn: 第一台电脑化文字处理器的创造者
bio: Evelyn Berezin was a computer engineer who built the first true computerized word processor. In 1969, she founded Redactron Corporation and designed the Data Secretary, a desk-sized machine that allowed users to edit, delete, and reformat text — capabilities taken for granted today but revolutionary at the time. Earlier in her career at Underwood Corporation and Teleregister, she helped design one of the first computer-based airline reservation systems for United Airlines. The New York Times called her 'the most important person in history that you've never heard of.'
bioCn: 伊芙琳·贝雷津是一位计算机工程师，创造了第一台真正的电脑化文字处理器。1969年，她创办了Redactron公司，设计了Data Secretary——一台桌子大小的机器，允许用户编辑、删除和重新格式化文本。这些功能在今天被视为理所当然，但在当时是革命性的。在她职业生涯早期，她在Teleregister公司帮助设计了联合航空公司最早的计算机订票系统之一。《纽约时报》称她为'你从未听说过的历史上最重要的人'。
achievements: Created the first computerized word processor (1969)
Founded Redactron Corporation
Helped design early airline reservation systems
achievementsCn: 创造了第一台电脑化文字处理器（1969年）
创办Redactron公司
帮助设计了早期航空订票系统
references: The New York Times obituary, December 2018

name: Dame Wendy Hall
taglineCn: 网络科学先驱
bio: Dame Wendy Hall is a British computer scientist who pioneered work in multimedia and hypermedia. In the late 1980s, her Microcosm system at the University of Southampton explored open hypermedia linking — ideas that predated and paralleled the development of the World Wide Web. She co-founded the Web Science Research Initiative with Tim Berners-Lee, establishing Web Science as an interdisciplinary academic field. She has served as President of the ACM, the first person outside North America to hold the position, and was appointed Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire.
bioCn: 温迪·霍尔女爵士是英国计算机科学家，在多媒体和超媒体领域做出了开创性工作。1980年代末，她在南安普顿大学的Microcosm系统探索了开放超媒体链接——这些理念早于并平行于万维网的发展。她与蒂姆·伯纳斯-李共同创办了网络科学研究倡议，将网络科学确立为一个跨学科学术领域。她曾担任ACM（美国计算机协会）主席——北美以外第一位担任此职务的人，并被授予大英帝国司令勋章。
achievements: Pioneered open hypermedia and the Microcosm system
Co-founded Web Science with Tim Berners-Lee
First non-North American ACM President
achievementsCn: 开创开放超媒体和Microcosm系统
与蒂姆·伯纳斯-李共同创立网络科学
首位非北美ACM主席

name: Katie Bouman
tagline: Led the Algorithm That Captured the First Black Hole Image
taglineCn: 领导算法捕捉首张黑洞图像
bio: Katherine Louise Bouman is a computer scientist and electrical engineer who led the development of a computational imaging algorithm that made it possible to capture the first-ever image of a black hole. As part of the Event Horizon Telescope project, she developed the CHIRP algorithm (Continuous High-resolution Image Reconstruction using Patch priors) to combine data from radio telescopes around the world. The resulting image of the black hole at the center of galaxy M87, released on April 10, 2019, was hailed as a landmark achievement. She is currently an assistant professor at Caltech.
bioCn: 凯瑟琳·路易斯·鲍曼是一位计算机科学家和电气工程师，她领导开发了使捕捉首张黑洞图像成为可能的计算成像算法。作为事件视界望远镜项目的一部分，她开发了CHIRP算法，将来自世界各地射电望远镜的数据组合在一起。2019年4月10日发布的M87星系中心黑洞图像被誉为里程碑式的成就。她目前是加州理工学院的助理教授。
achievements: Led development of black hole imaging algorithm
Key contributor to Event Horizon Telescope project
Assistant Professor at Caltech
achievementsCn: 领导开发黑洞成像算法
事件视界望远镜项目关键贡献者
加州理工学院助理教授
references: Bouman et al. Computational Imaging for VLBI Image Reconstruction. IEEE, 2016.

name: Chieko Asakawa
tagline: Pioneer of Digital Accessibility for the Visually Impaired
taglineCn: 视障人士数字无障碍先驱
bio: Chieko Asakawa is a computer scientist and IBM Fellow who has devoted her career to making technology accessible to the visually impaired — a mission born from personal experience, as she lost her sight at age 14 due to a swimming accident. At IBM Research in Tokyo, she developed the Home Page Reader, one of the first practical voice browsers for navigating the web. She also created the aDesigner accessibility simulation tool and leads research in AI-powered navigation for blind users. In 2019, she was inducted into the National Inventors Hall of Fame.
bioCn: 浅川智惠子是计算机科学家和IBM院士，她将职业生涯投入到使技术对视障人士无障碍的事业中——这一使命源于个人经历，她14岁时因游泳事故失明。在IBM东京研究院，她开发了Home Page Reader——最早的实用型网页语音浏览器之一。她还创建了aDesigner无障碍模拟工具，并领导AI辅助导航研究。2019年，她入选美国发明家名人堂。
achievements: Developed IBM Home Page Reader voice browser
IBM Fellow and accessibility technology pioneer
Inducted into National Inventors Hall of Fame (2019)
achievementsCn: 开发IBM Home Page Reader语音浏览器
IBM院士和无障碍技术先驱
入选美国发明家名人堂（2019年）
references: TED Talk: How new technology helps blind people explore the world, 2015

name: Elizabeth Blackburn
taglineCn: 发现端粒酶的诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Elizabeth Blackburn is a molecular biologist who discovered the molecular nature of telomeres — the protective caps at the ends of chromosomes — and co-discovered telomerase, the enzyme that replenishes them. This work revealed fundamental mechanisms of aging and cancer. She shared the 2009 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Carol Greider and Jack Szostak. Her research has opened vast new fields in aging biology, cancer research, and stress physiology. She served as President of the Salk Institute for Biological Studies.
bioCn: 伊丽莎白·布莱克本是一位分子生物学家，她发现了端粒——染色体末端保护帽——的分子本质，并共同发现了补充端粒的端粒酶。这项工作揭示了衰老和癌症的基本机制。她与卡罗尔·格雷德和杰克·绍斯塔克共同获得了2009年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。她的研究开辟了衰老生物学、癌症研究和应激生理学的广阔新领域。她曾担任索尔克生物研究所所长。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2009)
Discovered the molecular nature of telomeres
Co-discovered telomerase enzyme
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2009年）
发现端粒的分子本质
共同发现端粒酶
references: Blackburn, E. & Epel, E. The Telomere Effect. Grand Central Publishing, 2017.
